title: Certificate in Working with Children in Early Years and Primary Settings Level 2
overview: Childcare settings are always changing and this course is perfect for anyone wanting to understand the sector. You will cover a wide range of units and modules in our dedicated childcare space within the college, with access to valuable resources that will help inform your development. Our dedicated tutors will be on hand to provide a comprehensive and detailed approach to learning. Outside of the classroom, you will have significant opportunities to experience a range of different childcare settings for your work placement. Our dedicated Work Placement Team can ensure you get the placement beneficial to your career choice. This course will provide you with the knowledge, understanding and skills to support children’s physical care as part of a healthy lifestyle. You will be introduced to the role of the Childcare Practitioner in supporting children’s development within school settings, as well as learning about legislation and guidelines for the safeguarding, protection and welfare of children. You will be able to successfully put theory into practice which will enable you to successfully reflect upon practice within the workplace. You will be assessed in the work place and undertake a minimum of 130 hours work placement in a childcare setting.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 4 GCSEs with the following grades: English at 3 or above and maths at 2 or above and 2 other GCSEs at 3 or above. You will also need to have a DBS check.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: ApprenticeshipEarly Years Educator T Level Level 3
career_opportunities: This course will enable you to progress to the level 3 National Diploma which, combined with GCSEs grade 4 in English and maths, will grant you full, professional Early Years Educator status.
assessment: You will be continually assessed through written assignments, an external exam, group work and successful work placement portfolio.

title: Chef De Partie Apprenticeship Level 3 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.As an apprentice you will spend a minimum of 30 hours a week working in a kitchen and one day a week, usually a Monday, in college. This apprenticeship takes the skills you already have and develops them to support you to work in a more senior position in the kitchen. You will also look at the principals of food preparation and cooking, including traditional and modern cuisine, as well as looking at allergens, diet and nutrition. In a busy kitchen you will demonstrate that you can work under pressure as part of a team while preparing, cooking and finishing dishes and that you are competent in the handling and storage of ingredients. Whilst on this apprenticeship, you will be expected to develop a better understanding of the industry and food trends that influence the development of dishes and menus. You will demonstrate that you can maximise yield, quality and minimise waste in the kitchen. A Chef De Partie is expected to understand food safety practices and procedures, as well as being able to complete risk assessments. You will be taught the importance of team leading and communication in the kitchen. As part of your apprenticeship, you will also look at business strategy, understanding customer profiles, culture and constraints.
entry_requirements: A relevant level 2 qualification and GCSE English and maths grade 4 or above.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Hospitality Kitchen Management courseEmployment
career_opportunities: There are a range of different careers available to you after this apprenticeship. You could work in a professional kitchen in a range of settings including restaurants, hotels, and cruise ships.
assessment: The end point assessment is made up of a professional discussion, an observation in your workplace, completion of a set task and an online multiple-choice assessment. You will be expected to have passed your GCSE English and maths.
additional_information: Depending on your employer, you may be required to purchase a uniform and knife set. These can be purchased from college.

title: CISCO IT Essentials
overview: Imagine building a computer, then connecting it securely to a network. This is the exciting first step that could lead to a rewarding IT career. This course covers the skills needed for entry-level IT jobs. CISCO IT Essentials provides a better understanding of computer systems, preventing damage and assembling a computer system. Once you assemble the computer, you will learn how to install the software, networking and troubleshooting. Use the CISCO Learning Academy course materials to work at your own pace and improve your knowledge of computers. Gain a better understanding of mobile technologies, configuration, diagnostics and security threats, such as malware, phishing and social engineering. The course is to be delivered predominantly online, but there are hands-on elements of the course, which require you to complete tasks practically as well as theoretically.
fees_funding: This course costs £380.
assessment: Online assessments

title: Coaching and Mentoring Award Level 3
overview: The Level 3 Award in Coaching and Mentoring course introduces individuals to the roles and responsibilities of coaches and mentors. You must acquire the qualities and abilities to be effective in this role. It is a process that will require you to develop yourself and become confident in your ability to improve performance in the workplace. The course aims to develop mentoring skills to achieve the objectives of individuals and teams. Studying this course will give you the opportunity to firstly, know what it takes to be an effective coach/mentor in the workplace. You will also learn how to carry out supervised coaching sessions and improve your own coaching abilities by analysing, assessing and planning. During this course you will be able to put your newly acquired skills into practice. You will learn how to understand the impact on the individual and team needs of the coaching and mentoring process, you will understand the knowledge, skills or behaviours of individuals. You will also gain an understanding about what the individual and team need from coaching and mentoring and the construction of a suitable process to meet those needs. This course is completed in a time scale agreed with your tutor. There are numerous start dates throughout the year for this course.
entry_requirements: This qualification is open to all learners without any restrictions. You don’t need to have Coaching/Mentoring background to study this. You just need to be able to demonstrate a good level of written English.
fees_funding: The cost of this course is £360. Payment can be via instalments.To find out if you are eligible for funding, please email customerservices@kirkleescollege.ac.uk
progression: Coaching and Mentoring Certificate, and then Coaching and Mentoring Diploma.
career_opportunities: Upon successful completion of this Award in Coaching and Mentoring , you will be able to demonstrate desirable skills, which will help you gain employment
assessment: Completion of an online workbook. You may have online assessment to sit for this course.

title: Coaching and Mentoring Certificate Level 3
overview: The Certificate in Coaching and Mentoring is designed to introduce learners about the types, nature, tools, methodologies and complex nature of coaching and mentoring. This course provides in-depth skills, knowledge and understanding of coaching and mentoring tools, theory and methodologies. This course will mean you can work in any sector to apply your skills as a coach or a mentor. By the end of the Certificate in Coaching and Mentoring, you should be able to, understand methodologies and models of coaching and mentoring. You will also be able to apply suitable tools, models or methodology in your role. You should also be able to identify key issues within coaching and mentoring. Finally you will be able to evaluate and review your own ability and performance as a coach or mentor. On this course you will learn and study a range of topics including, how to communicate and deliver a client centred approach, how to listen to the client and stakeholders, how to prepare and deliver a coaching and mentoring session, and how to build a client relationship and establish SMART goals for the client. You will be able to conclude and evaluate the relationship and reflect on your own ethics and processes. For further information please contact info@kirkleescollege.ac.uk
entry_requirements: This qualification is open to all learners without any restrictions. You don’t need to have Coaching/Mentoring background to study this.
fees_funding: The cost of this course is £765. Payment can be via instalments.To find out if you are eligible for funding, please email customerservices@kirkleescollege.ac.uk
career_opportunities: Certification holders work as a first line manager, supervisors, trainers, teachers, consultants, coach, mentor, or a counsellor in any organisation (public or private sector).
assessment: Multiple choice questions and assignment

meta_description: Learn the skills to coach people as part of your normal working role. A short certificate course for all professionals.

title: Coaching and Mentoring Diploma Level 3
overview: The Level 3 Diploma in Coaching and Mentoring is designed for supervisors and first line managers. It builds on the learning gained in both the Award and Certificate in Coaching and Mentoring. You will learn the roles and responsibilities of a coach or a mentor, the techniques of understanding your clients, and giving feedback will be taught and discussed. You will discover the attractive characteristics of a coach and mentor and learn how to provide effective feedback while maintaining the trust. Importantly you will study how to complete the coaching and mentoring cycle and complete the necessary evaluation of the activity. On this course you will study the purpose of coaching and mentoring and how it differs from each other. You will learn how to better coach employees to meet desired results and understand how to use the SMART technique to set appropriate goals. During further study you will examine the benefits of fostering good working relationships, and explore the various obstacles that could stop the development and growth of an employee. You complete this course at your own pace to an agreed deadline with your tutor. This course has mutiple starts throughout the year.To register your interest please call 01484 437070
fees_funding: This course costs £1,214.Further information about the fee, and any support towards paying it, can be obtained from customerservices@kirkleescollege.ac.uk
progression: Level 5 qualification in Coaching and Mentoring
career_opportunities: You qualification can support you in a variety of roles including management.
assessment: Multiple choice questions and assignment

title: Commis Chef Apprenticeship Level 2 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.Commis chef is the starting point in most kitchens. On your apprenticeship, you will spend a minimum of 30 hours working in industry and one day a week at college. In college, you will spend time in the kitchens with our chef lecturers developing your cooking skills and also working on your English and maths portfolios if required. All apprentices will also get the chance to develop hot and cold desserts, produce cakes, biscuits and bread. By the end of your apprenticeship, you will have developed the correct techniques with knives and how to use a variety of kitchen tools and equipment. In the modern kitchen it is important to accommodate a range of specialist diets and understand the risks of allergies. This apprenticeship will teach you the basic skills you need to be a commis chef in a busy kitchen covering all stations. You will learn how to prepare, cook and finish basic dishes, including fish and shellfish, poultry, meat and game, as well as learning to prepare vegetables. You will spend time mastering the skills required to make stocks and sauces, soups and egg dishes. An important element of your apprenticeship will be understanding health, safety and security in the workplace and waste management. All chefs need to plan a menu, budget it and understand supply management. A well-run kitchen involves teamwork and this is a key part of your apprenticeship, as well as understanding the expected professional behaviour in the industry.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths at grade 3 or above.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Level 3 Apprenticeship Chef De PartieEmployment
assessment: To complete your apprenticeship, you need to complete the end point assessment. This involves passing your English and maths qualification at level 1 and show that you are working towards level 2. You will also have to complete a professional discussion, be observed in the workplace, complete a set task and an online multiple-choice assessment.

title: Confectionary Craft Taster
overview: Welcome to our Confectionary Craft! Perfect for beginners, this class will teach you how to make salted caramel muffins. We provide all the ingredients you need, so you can focus on learning without the hassle of shopping. Our experienced chef will guide you through each step, showing you how to create light and fluffy salted caramel muffins that you can take home and enjoy. Don’t miss this opportunity to learn the basics of patisserie. This session will take place on Wednesdays 21st and 28th January, 2.30- 5.30pm and if you enjoy the class, you can join us for the full Introduction to Patisserie course that will start the following week. This course is designed for those with little to no baking experience. Our chef lecturer will be there to help you every step of the way, ensuring you gain confidence in mixing, baking and decorating. At the end of the course, you’ll get to take home your freshly baked creations.Class times: Wednesdays 14:30-17:30

title: Construction Equipment Maintenance Technician Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: The apprenticeship will aim to give you the knowledge and skills to become a successful construction equipment maintenance technician and help you achieve a qualification that will enable you to start on the path to an exciting career in an evolving industry. As an apprentice technician, you will carry out important tasks such as installing, removing, upgrading, modifying, and adapting equipment. With the increasing use of advanced electronic systems, hybrid technology, and cleaner equipment, you will start learning how to adapt to new and developing technologies linked to low and zero-carbon machinery. You will work with a wide range of construction equipment, including complex fixed machinery and mobile plant. You will need to understand electronic control systems as well as the mechanical and chemical processes involved. Your role is important in keeping construction and related equipment working safely and efficiently. You will be able to have a direct impact on the delivery of infrastructure projects and housing developments.
entry_requirements: An appropriate Level 2 qualification, and a minimum of grade 4 in GCSE English and maths.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
assessment: Practical assessments, Online knowledge assessments, written knowledge assessments.An apprentice will typically spend 36 months on-programme working towards the apprenticeship standard. The EPA takes place at the end of the apprentice’s learning and development, after the apprentice has met the EPA Gateway requirements.

overview: On this Level 1, you will be based at our Brunel Construction Centre in Huddersfield, where you will learn introductory skills in a range of construction trades. Our tutors will individually assess you before you start the course to see which level you will be working at. This course is perfect if you want to pursue a career in construction but have not yet decided which trade you want to specialise in. You will get an opportunity to try your hand at all the main trade areas including plastering, joinery, painting and decorating and brickwork. Our tutors are all experts in their areas and have years of experience in their trade to pass on, so you will be learning from the best and have access to all the necessary equipment and tools. As a Level 1 student at college, you will have English and maths GCSE lessons if you have not already gained a grade 4 in these qualifications and also have personal development, digital skills and employability skills sessions built into your timetable.

title: Construction Plant or Machinery Maintenance Diploma Level 3
overview: This qualification enables you, the learner, to demonstrate and recognise your skills, knowledge and understanding so you can work as a Plant Mechanic within the construction industry.You will be learning at our brand-new Plant Machinery site at Engineering Centre, learning in a real-world environment, on real state-of-the-art plant machinery equipment. This course has been designed with employers to meet relevant industry standards. You will be assessed against a set of performance and knowledge statements which have been derived from National Occupational Standards for your occupational area. This course is made up of 12 mandatory units, including operating plant or machinery for non-operational maintenance activities; diagnosing faults in plant or machinery; thermal cutting and joining materials; producing one-off components by bench fitting techniques; installing plant or machinery; configuring and handing over plant or machinery; planning and carrying out servicing to maintain plant or machinery, and advising on repair or replacement for plant or machinery.
entry_requirements: You will need a grade 4/C in GCSE English and maths. You must have previous experience working in the Motor Vehicle industry to start this course. If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
fees_funding: This course costs £3,345 for learners aged 19+.
assessment: Assessment of learners’ knowledge and understanding will be undertaken through practical assignment and question-based activities undertaken in the training environment.

This course is designed for the next generation of creators, those who don’t just play games but build audiences, shape online communities, and produce professional-level content across multiple platforms. You won’t just learn theory, you will research, plan, and produce real esports content, simulating industry workflows and building a portfolio that reflects current professional standards. title: Corporate Responsibility and Sustainability Apprenticeship Level 4 (September 2025 Entry)
overview: This is one of the apprenticeships commissioned to commemorate the coronation of His Majesty King Charles III. This apprenticeship will train you to take on responsibility for sustainable practices and solutions within a business or organisation. Job titles may include coordinator and administrator roles related to corporate sustainability responsibility. A major component of the apprenticeship is learning how to develop strategies that manage social environmental and ethical risks and opportunities while improving reputation and branding. You will also learn about the principles of data management including how to manipulate and store data needed for CR&S projects. Over the 36-month period, you will attend face to face lessons at least once a month at Kirklees College’s Huddersfield Centre to acquire the knowledge, skills and behaviours needed to execute your role at work. During the course, you will cover a range of topics including project planning, communication and managing stakeholder relationships, data analysis, quality assurance and budget management.
entry_requirements: Companies will set their own individual entry requirements. However, as this is a level 4 apprenticeship, students will be expected to have appropriate level 3 qualifications, either a BTEC Extended Diploma, A Levels or a level 3 apprenticeship. Ideally the suitable candidate will hold GCSE English and maths grade 4/C or above.To start your apprenticeship, you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
assessment: When you have completed all compulsory elements of the course you will begin to prepare for your End-Point Assessment (EPA). You submit a work-based project report relevant to the needs of your employer and you will also engage in a professional discussion based on a portfolio of evidence you have compiled.

title: Counselling Skills Level 2
overview: This course is accredited by the awarding body SEG and will give you the opportunity to try out a number of basic counselling skills. You will develop and understanding of ethical practice, boundaries issues and learn some counselling theory. You will also explore effective communication skills used in helping relationships. You will demonstrate a range of listening and responding skills to establish and develop close interactions. You will study the professional context of counselling. On this unit you will develop an understanding of what counselling is. You will learn about the importance of creating the right environment and the right ethos for the helping relationship to be successful. Towards the end of this course you will have the opportunity to reflect on your personal development and identify areas for improvement in the future.
entry_requirements: There are no formal entry requirements but a good level of English is required as this course is part assessed on your written work. You must complete every section of the college’s application form and pre-interview task to a satisfactory standard.

title: Craft Bricklayer Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2025 Entry)
overview: Craft bricklayers typically work in the new build, refurbishment and heritage sectors and work on specialist projects, including work on listed buildings. They are masters in their field and by starting this apprenticeship, you can become one too. Before starting your apprenticeship, you will need to secure suitable employment, which could be with a smaller firm, or large company. You will also learn about the wider construction industry and gain skills required to collaborate with wider tradespeople, such as site carpenters and roofers. During your apprenticeship, you will learn how to set out and lay bricks, brick specials, blocks and complex walls, plus features such as chimneys and decorative masonry work. You will also develop the professional skills required to take the lead on site as a technical expert, as craft bricklayers are often responsible for delivering the masonry elements of construction projects. You will also be required to undertake thorough health and safety training to prepare you for working safely on site. Your college sessions will be delivered in our workshops at Brunel Construction Centre in Huddersfield.
entry_requirements: An appropriate Level 2 qualification, and GCSE English and maths at grade 3 or above.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Employment
assessment: You will undergo End Point Assessment at the end of your apprenticeship. This could include a multiple-choice test, practical assessment with questions and an interview underpinned by a portfolio of evidence.

title: Craft Carpentry and Joinery Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
qualification: L3 APPRENTICESHIP STANDARD IN ADVANCED CARPENTRY AND JOINERY - ARCHITECTURAL
overview: Currently there is a high demand for carpenters and joiners within the construction industry to help meet the skills need for the transition to modern methods of construction, heritage projects and sustainable building, so there has never been a better time to start this apprenticeship. You will also learn about the wider construction trades so you can effectively liaise with bricklayers, plasterers, plumbers, supervisors, site management, architects, designers, customers and contractors. You will learn how to make a range of building components from wood and associated materials. This apprenticeship occupation has two possible job roles: Advanced Site Carpenter and Advanced Architectural Joiners. Advanced Site Carpenters will work on irregular and complex structures, including roofs, and Advanced Architectural Joiners create complex building components by setting out, marking out and create assembled products requiring advanced skilled work including the full range of woodworking machines. You will also be required to undertake thorough health and safety training to prepare you for working safely on site. Your college sessions will be delivered in our workshops at Brunel Construction Centre in Huddersfield.

title: Creative Media Level 2
overview: You will learn the skills required to create digital content for a fast-paced social media market and work across a variety of formats to create content for all aspects of online media. Your focus will be on developing both your creative and digital technical skills with industry standard software packages. We will help you develop your verbal, written and visual communication skills so you understand the needs of your client to help you to formulate, develop and pitch your own ideas. You will spend up to 15 hours a week developing practical skills, such as video, radio, photography and animation. You will study through a programme of skills-based workshops and supporting classes, which will introduce you to issues and debates within the media industry. You will study the different digital media sectors and audiences and the range of technological platforms used to distribute media. For example, how each sector has a common production process and planning and pitching a digital media product.

course_code: L2 DIPLOMA IN CREATIVE MEDIA SKILLS
meta_description: This course is a fantastic introduction to a career in the creative digital media industries.

title: Creative Media Practice Level 3
overview: You will be equipped with the skills to create digital content for the fast-paced digital market and work across a variety of formats to create content for all aspects on online media. This course will develop your skills in visual and written communication, including film and video, photography, publishing, broadcasting, editing and journalism to help you pursue a career in media. You will expand your knowledge across a broad spectrum of media topics, including film, animation, podcasts, graphic design, publishing, and photography. As you progress, you can focus on an area or areas of media that relate to your personal interests. The course supports you in developing your skills for creative intention through developing research projects, mood boards, storyboards and recording your reflection and critical thinking. Your personal and transferable skills are also developed through team working, working to meet a prescribed brief, working to deadlines, presenting ideas and information effectively and accurately completing administrative tasks and processes. The course aims to cover a broad variety of media skills and knowledge and give you the best opportunity to progress to higher education or a professional working environment. Workshops and seminars are supplemented by visits and workshops led by specialist media practitioners. You will also gain first-hand experience in the creative media industry by learning how to respond to live client briefs.

title: Customer Service Practitioner Apprenticeship Level 2 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.As a Customer Service apprentice, you need to be employed for at least 30 hours a week and will attend college for one day a week. Good customer service is key to the successful of any business or organisation. As a customer service apprentice, you will probably work front of house in an employer’s business activities and regularly assist customers. Your apprenticeship comprises of numerous units, including employee rights and responsibilities. Within the Customer Service Practitioner qualification, you will cover business areas such as meeting regulations and legislation systems and resources. You will be expected to demonstrate your organisational skills and how you would deal with customers’ conflict and challenge. Duties will vary between sectors but your main role will be making sure that customers are dealt with in a positive, reliable and pleasant way whether that is by offering advice, answering questions or handling complaints. To properly assist customers, you will need clear and up-to-date knowledge of your organisation’s products and services. You will also need to communicate with a variety of people. You will be expected to provide evidence of how you have developed and completed a portfolio of work. You will be expected to show how you work as part of a team and how you receive feedback. As part of your apprenticeship, you will also study English and maths at level 1 (if not achieved already.)
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths grade 3 or above.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Level 3 Customer Service apprenticeship or another business administration areaEmployment
assessment: You will need to complete a portfolio of work, take part in a workplace observation and a professional discussion. You will also be required to complete a synoptic test. You must pass English and maths at GCSE as part of this course.

title: Cyber Security Technician Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Start)
overview: Cyber Security Technicians provide the first line of cyber security response for any organisation. This role involves collaborating internally and externally to support secure business operations by monitoring and detecting potential threats and escalating them when necessary. You will learn how to deliver a security culture across an organisation, understand vulnerabilities and threats and support the development of an organisation’s cyber security plan. You will learn how to install procedures and technical security controls in accordance with relevant standards to maintain an organisation’s security. You will also be the help desk point of contact and process requests ensuring confidentiality and integrity. You will monitor compliance, identify security incidents and escalate these when necessary, as well as provide relevant data to auditors if required. You will study how to conduct specific cyber security tasks in line with relevant procedures and standards. You will have to implement specific cyber security mechanisms and controls, including patching software, installing software updates, implementing access control, configuring firewalls, security incident and event management tools (SIEM) tools and protection tools (anti-virus, anti-malware, anti-spam). From your experience of applying policies, you will review cyber security policies in line with the appropriate guidelines. You will take responsibility for your own self-development by keeping up to date with industry trends and developments to enhance your skills. You must have a suitable employer to be able to start an apprenticeship and be employed for a minimum of 30 hours a week.
entry_requirements: GCSE grade 4 or above in maths and English. To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Level 4 apprenticeship in fields of networks and cybersecurityUniversity Employment
assessment: You will be assessed through practical, observations, exams, projects and end point assessment.

title: Early Years Educator Apprenticeship Level 3 (October 2026 Entry)
overview: As an apprentice Early Years Educator apprentice, you may work in a range of settings, including full day care, children’s centres, preschools and reception classes. You may either be working on your own or supervising others to deliver the Early Years Foundation Stage (EYFS) requirements set by government for the learning, development and care of children from birth to 5 years old. The course will cover various subjects related to child development, safeguarding, play, learning and assessment. You will be expected to demonstrate and apply your knowledge and skills on placement and also within your own individual portfolio. You will take part in a variety of planned placement activities over the 18-month programme. You will be expected to communicate and interact effectively within a team using a full range of skills. You will learn the importance of working independently, taking responsibility for initiating and completing tasks and understanding the impact of your work on others within an Early Education setting. Individuals will undergo all the checks as per the EYFS (Early Years Foundation Stage) requirements to ensure suitability to work with children. Face to Face delivery will take place in college on the first Thursday of the month. Calendar dates will be provided on sign up.
entry_requirements: Level 2 or grade 4 and above in 5 subjects which must include maths and English Language. You can be 16 years old to apply for this apprenticeship but it is at the discretion of the employer whether they are willing to hire a 16-year-old apprentice. To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Foundation degree in Early Years Education or another course in Higher Education Full-time employment
career_opportunities: · Early Years Manager · Early Years Placement Supervisor
assessment: The end-point assessment for this apprenticeship involves a professional discussion and knowledge test. You will be required to complete a portfolio of work and a variety of placement observations.

course_code: L3 APPRENTICESHIP STANDARD - EARLY YEARS EDUCATOR
meta_description: The Early Years Educator Apprenticeship provides you with the knowledge, skills and behaviours to become a highly trained professional who will play a key role in ensuring that young children learn and develop well and are kept healthy and safe.

title: Early Years Practitioner Apprenticeship Level 2 (October 2026 Entry)
overview: As an apprentice Early Years Practitioner, you will be responsible for supporting child-initiated and adult-led activities based around the needs and interests of each individual child. You will support children’s learning through planned, purposeful play opportunities and educational programmes and working as part of a team to ensure each child feels safe and secure. During the qualification, you will have the opportunity to put these skills into practice with at least 12 months of workplace experience in the industry. Your assessor will arrange a variety of workplace observations to assess your practice. As an apprentice Early Years Practitioner, you will also support the observation and assessment of children and contribute to their learning experiences and assist with the care needs of the individual child. English and maths Level 2 Functional Skills forms a mandatory part of this apprenticeship and must be achieved before end-point assessment. Face to Face delivery in college will take place on the last Thursday of the month. Calendar dates will be provided on sign up.
entry_requirements: Grade 3 and above in English Language and three other GCSEs at grade 3 and above. You can be 16 years old to apply for this apprenticeship but it is at the discretion of the employer whether they are willing to hire a 16-year-old apprentice. To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Early Years Educator T Level Level 3Foundation degree in Early Years Education Full-time employment
career_opportunities: Early Years Practitioner
assessment: The end-point assessment for this apprenticeship involves a professional discussion and knowledge test. You will be required to complete a portfolio of work and a variety of placement observation to enable you to progress onto Level 3 or achieve a Level 2 Licence to Practice.

title: Electrical Installation Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: To be an apprentice you need to be working for a minimum of 30 hours a week and be able to attend college one day a week. Installation Electricians work on the installation, testing, commissioning and maintenance of low voltage (less than 1000v) electrical and electronic devices and appliances. You might work in both indoor and outdoor settings. Our tutors will teach you how to connect and disconnect conductors, cables and cords in electrical systems and how to diagnose and correct electrical faults in electro technical systems and equipment in buildings, structures and the environment. You will also be expected to understand and apply the electrical principles associated with the design, building, installation and maintenance of electrical equipment and systems. On your apprenticeship you will use engineering knowledge and understanding to apply technical and practical skills. You will contribute to the design, development, manufacture, construction, commissioning, operation or maintenance of products, equipment, processes, systems or services. By the end of your apprenticeship you will understand the practices and procedures for the preparation and installation of wiring systems and electro technical equipment in buildings, structures and the environment. Health and Safety is an important element of your apprenticeship. You will learn to understand and apply health and safety and environmental regulations, guidance notes and relevant codes of practice; and the requirements of current Wiring Regulations.As part of your apprenticeship you will develop your communication skills. All apprentices learn about the importance of working in a way that contributes to sustainability and about environmental legislation.
entry_requirements: A minimum of GCSE English and maths at grade 4 or above.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Level 4 qualification, professional qualification or full time employment.
assessment: To successfully complete your apprenticeship you must pass an on-line assessment, a portfolio of work, complete a skills test and undertake a professional discussion.

course_instances.code: L3 APPRENTICESHIP STANDARD IN ELECTRICAL INSTALLATION

course_code: L3 APPRENTICESHIP STANDARD IN ELECTRICAL INSTALLATION

title: Electrical Installation Level 1
location: 2 LocationsBrunel Construction CentreSpringfield Sixth Form Centre

raw_meta.where: 2 LocationsBrunel Construction CentreSpringfield Sixth Form Centre

overview: This course provides an introduction to electrical installation and is a mixture of practical lessons in our workshops, alongside theory-based classroom sessions. Classroom sessions are designed to help reinforce the skills you have learnt in the workshops and to develop a greater understanding of the trade. Your tutors will teach you how to plan, prepare for and carry out hand fitting activities. You will learn how to produce a work plan containing job instructions, materials, tools, equipment and components required to complete an activity. It is also important that you are aware of how to check your work for accuracy and good workmanship and keep you work area safe and tidy. On this course, you will gain a broad understanding of the electrical trade. As part of your course, you will look at tools and fixings used in the installation process. This can be different types of cable and flex, socket outlets and main earthing terminal. You will learn the difference between circuit breakers and fuses. It is important that you understand health and safety in the workplace. You will learn about the relevant legislation in the Electrical industry and how to apply it your work. You will also learn how to work safely whilst protecting the environment. This can include finding out about sustainability in the domestic industry, including energy efficient lighting and equipment.
entry_requirements: A minimum of GCSE English and maths grade 2 or above.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Electrical Installation Level 2Apprenticeship
career_opportunities: After your studies you can progress to be an electrician either in a company working a facilities team, or in a small firm.
assessment: You will be assessed by a mix of practical tasks, completed assignments, work and some online testing.
additional_information: You will need safety boots to be able to complete this course. If you are colour blind, please speak to your tutor as this may prevent you from entering the electrical installation trade.

meta_description: This qualification is your starting point to become an electrician. It is designed for new entrants to the electrical industry, providing you with the basic skills and experience needed to progress.

title: Electrical Installation Level 2
location: 2 LocationsBrunel Construction CentreSpringfield Sixth Form Centre

raw_meta.where: 2 LocationsBrunel Construction CentreSpringfield Sixth Form Centre

overview: This qualification focuses on practical skills and knowledge, combining classroom and practical workshop sessions. You will gain practical experience and an understanding of the electrical installation sector. It is important that you understand health and safety regulations and how to apply them to your work. This is both for your own safety, the safety of your colleagues and for your customers. You will be taught the current legislation and rules applying to the electrical installation trade. In our workshops, you will develop a working knowledge of installation and maintenance of wiring systems and enclosures, including more advanced practical skills, such as inspection and testing, and learn how to work with a range of materials such as steel and plastic conduit, trunking and PVC cables. On the course, you will also learn about different electrical installation technology and learn the principles of electrical science. You will also spend time learning about renewable energies and the use of solar systems in the industry.

title: Electrical Installation Level 3
overview: This qualification allows you to acquire advanced underpinning knowledge and related skills of electrical installation. The qualification does not require occupational evidence so is suitable if you currently do not work in the electrical industry but wish to progress into employment or higher education. You will understand installation containment and electrical principles. You will also learn about installation and technology, as well as fault diagnosis and how to rectify any fault you may encounter. This course will provide you with the essential practical skills and underpinning knowledge to become fully qualified in the electrical industry. You will learn about health and safety and wiring regulations so you can practice safely in the industry. You will also understand inspection and testing. The course has a successful track record of students progressing from this course into employment within the building services sector. There are a number of job opportunities within the sector currently due to the skills shortage. An estimated 168,500 jobs will be created over the next five years and labour market intelligence predicts Yorkshire and Humber need to recruit 10,000 new workers to meet demand.

meta_description: This advanced course is ideal if you wish to train as an electrician but are not currently working within the industry.

title: Engineering and Manufacturing Level 1
overview: This introductory level course will introduce you to a range of different engineering and manufacturing disciplines. You will develop skills in cutting, forming, machining and assembling engineering materials and building electronic circuits. This course also includes an introduction to the maths and science principles that are essential to engineering studies. You will study this course in our Engineering Centre, based in Huddersfield. This is a purpose-built centre and is used for the training of engineering and manufacturing students. It boasts excellent facilities with the latest equipment in industry standard workshops. This introductory course will also enable you to develop skills in managing your study and your approach to learning, along with how to prepare for a career in engineering. You will continue to study GCSE English and maths alongside this course. This study programme will also enable you to engage with local employers through a range of activities, including industrial visits, guest speakers and mock interviews. The course may also include at least 30 hours of work experience, where you will be placed with a local engineering company to gain some ‘real world’ experience.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 4 GCSEs with the following grades: English at 2 or above and maths at 2 or above and 2 other GCSEs at 2 or above ideally including a science or technology course.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Engineering and Manufacturing Level 2
career_opportunities: You could take up a position as trainee technician in computer aided draughting and design, mechanical and electronic fault diagnosis and repair.
assessment: There is a combination of practical assessments, written assessments and in-class exams.
additional_information: You will be required to wear all necessary personal protective equipment provided by the college when working in our engineering workshops.

You will also learn how to work efficiently, effectively and comply with all health and safety requirements whilst working in an engineering and manufacturing environment. This study programme will also enable you to engage with local employers through a range of activities, including industrial visits, guest speakers and mock interviews. title: Engineering and Manufacturing Level 3
overview: This course will help you develop your knowledge in mathematics and further mathematics for engineering technicians, material science, mechanical principles, electrical and electronic principles, machining processes and using programmable controllers for automation. You will learn how to produce engineering drawings using CAD. This course will also instruct you on how to work efficiently, effectively and comply with all health and safety requirements whilst working in an engineering and manufacturing workplace. You will study this course in our Engineering Centre, based in Huddersfield. This is a purpose-built centre and is used for the training of engineering and manufacturing students. It boasts excellent facilities with the latest equipment in industry standard workshops. On completion of the first year, you will be qualified to Engineering Technician Standard. At this point you will have a level 3 qualification which can be used to meet the requirements of the Engineering Technician apprenticeship in Mechatronics. However, if you want to go into higher education you must complete the second year. On completion of the second year, you will be sufficiently qualified to gain entry on our HNC/HND course or on a university BEng programme. This study programme will also enable you to engage with local employers through a range of activities, including industrial visits, guest speakers and mock interviews. The course will also include at least 30 hours of work experience, where you will be placed with a local engineering company to gain some ‘real world’ experience.

title: Engineering Fitter Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
start_date: This course has multiple start dates.

raw_meta.start_date: This course has multiple start dates.

overview: As an apprentice you will be employed for a minimum of 30 hours a week and attend college one day a week. You will also be expected to complete a block placement in college at the start of your apprenticeship. Typically, fitters may specialise in either mechanical, electrical, electronic, control systems, pipe fitting or instrumentation. Their role is multi-faceted and can operate across multiple disciplines depending on the type of assembly that is required. You will learn how to produce or re-furbish components, interpret drawings and specifications and plan your work accordingly. You will be expected to demonstrate that you can produce, refurbish, assemble and adjust components to a high standard. You will gain the skills needed to read and interpret component and assembly specifications, as well as how to prepare your work area, carry out relevant planning, measure and inspect components and assembly. This will include analysing and fixing issues where appropriate. You will learn how to apply improvement techniques and communicate with colleagues and customers, as well as complete component and assembly documentation. As an apprentice, you will learn the importance of health and safety regulations and how to apply them to your role. You will also learn how you contribute to the business by identifying possible opportunities for improving working practices, processes and/or procedures workplace.

title: Engineering Technician (Mechatronics Maintenance) Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: As an apprentice you will be employed for a minimum of 30 hours a week and attend college one day a week. You will also be expected to complete a block placement in college at the start of your apprenticeship. Mechatronics Maintenance Technicians ensure that plant and equipment perform to the required standard to facilitate production targets regarding safety, quality, delivery and cost within high value manufacturing environments. Our tutors will teach you how to carry out complex fault diagnosis and repair activities on high technology engineered systems such as mechanical equipment, electrical and electronic equipment and process control equipment. A good apprentice will know how to minimise machinery downtime by implementing planned preventative maintenance programmes. They will also be able to read and interpret relevant data and documentation used to maintain components, equipment and systems. Your apprenticeship will cover a broad range of activities include installation, testing, fault finding and the ongoing planned maintenance of complex automated equipment. This requires the application of a complex blend of skills, knowledge and occupational behaviours across the electrical, electronic, mechanical, fluid power and control systems disciplines.You will learn mechanical, electrical, electronic, fluid power and process control principles in a mechatronics maintenance environment. As an apprentice, you will learn the importance of health and safety regulations and how to apply them to your role. You will also learn how you contribute to the business by identifying possible opportunities for improving working practices, processes and/or procedures workplace.

title: Engineering Technician (Technical Support) Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: Technical Support Technicians provide technical support and expertise for engineering and manufacturing services, including communications software, test, analysis tools, measurement, off line programming, process control, performance and continuous improvement solutions, capacity planning, production scheduling/planning, product technical applications and capability, technical sales and marketing support, product development and innovation, engineering drawing, purchasing and/or supply of goods or services for engineering activities, quality control, inspection and e-commerce technologies. You will learn to produce and amend technical documentation that contains all the relevant and necessary data required. You will then present this in the required format and ensure that codes, symbols and other references used follow agreed conventions. You will learn mathematical techniques, formula and calculations, as well as the methods and techniques used to evaluate technical data and documentation and how to identify that the data and documentation being used is current and up to date. You will learn how to make changes to issued documentation, how to source other areas of technical expertise/information and understand the requirements of the customer and support them appropriately. As an apprentice, you will follow assurance control procedures, develop effective business and/or customer relationships, provide technical advice and guidance to others and contribute to the business by identifying possible opportunities for improving working practices, processes and/or procedures.

title: English For Speakers of Other Languages (ESOL)
overview: On this course, you will study English, maths and digital skills to prepare and equip you with the right tools and skills for the workplace. You will learn a wide range of real-life texts and resources to help you with the skills to live, work and communicate effectively in English and develop your confidence so that you can become a confident and successful citizen of the UK. You will also develop your confidence in maths, such as using numbers and the numeric system in the UK. You will learn how to handle data and describe trends, as well as learn measuring, shape and space skills. You will learn how to use theinternet for research and leisure and how to stay safe online. On this course you will develop essential skills such as problem solving and being a team member, an understanding of UK culture and society, self-awareness and confidence. With a focus on studying in a real-life context, you will read everyday texts and gain an understanding of everyday grammar and vocabulary to write in a clear and effective way. You will also build your communication skills in a range of familiar settings. You will have the opportunity to undertake work experience on this course. You will be supported in researching careers related to your personal interests in order to identify a suitable placement.
entry_requirements: This course is for young people aged 16 – 18. There are no formal entry requirements for this course. You will take part in an interview including an initial assessment prior to being enrolled on the right level of course.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Vocational course once you have gained Entry Level 3 Functional Skills
assessment: Exams will be taken at the end of the academic year.
additional_information: An ESOL course is individual to your own personal needs, this is why we ask all students to complete an assessment so that we can work out the right course level for you.

title: Esports Level 3
qualification: FL3 DIPLOMA IN CREATIVE MEDIA PRODUCTION & TECHNOLOGY (CONTENT CREATION FOR ESPORTS)
overview: Esports is an industry where teams will compete against one another using video games. The industry has both local and global stages, which can be as small as a school holding a private match or as large as two countries holding tournaments that span a few days. There are many new career avenues to explore in the industry either through being part of a competing team or competition hosts. As part of the course, you will undertake a mixture of practical and theory-based tasks. You will understand the business application of Esports by creating your own team and designing and marketing your own logo and merchandise. You will also plan your own Esports event and understand nutrition for performance by designing a workout and diet plan. In your first year, you will be introduced to Esports and learn the key skills and strategies that can be used in games. In your second year, you will learn about the health and wellbeing for Esports players, as well as social responsibility and current ethical issues in the industry. You will also develop your game design knowledge by developing your own Esports game. You will have access to the college’s brand-new dedicated room which will provide high-spec gaming computers and equipment in a fun and inspiring environment. You will also have the opportunity to meet guest speakers from the industry and attend a variety of competitions both in and outside of college.
entry_requirements: 5 GCSEs grade 4 or above including English and maths. If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: University, employment or an apprenticeship.
career_opportunities: Esports industry, IT, Public services, Cyber security, Journalist, Streamer.
assessment: You will be assessed through written assignments, projects and controlled assessments.

title: Fabrication and Welding Programme Level 2
overview: This Level 2 course will develop the practical skills you will require to gain an apprenticeship in Fabrication and welding. You will develop skills in cutting, forming, welding and assembling welded and fabricated components/materials. You will study this course in our Engineering Centre, based in Huddersfield. This is a purpose-built centre and is used for the training of engineering and manufacturing students. It boasts excellent welding facilities with the latest equipment in industry standard workshops. You will develop skills in TIG welding (Tungsten Inert Gas Welding), MIG welding (Metal Inert Gas Welding), sheet metal work, engineering drawing, health and safety and communications. This study programme will also enable you to engage with local employers through a range of activities, including industrial visits, guest speakers and mock interviews. The course will also include at least 30 hours of work experience, where you will be placed with a local engineering company to gain some ‘real world’ experience.
entry_requirements: A minimum of grade 3 in maths and English. If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.

assessment: There is a combination of practical assessments, written assessments and in-class exams.
additional_information: You will be required to wear all necessary personal protective equipment provided by the college when working in our engineering workshops.

title: Fashion and Textiles Level 3
overview: During this Level 3 course, you will study the fashion industry and textile making techniques in depth to develop your personal creative practice and portfolio. This will prepare you for higher level study and a career in the industry. You will also develop your textile making skills, including design for interiors and accessories. Throughout the course, you will work with your Personal Development Coach to find suitable work placement opportunities which will give you the vital experience needed to work in this competitive and demanding industry. Fashion skills you will develop include pattern cutting, dressmaking, digital content creation and design. You will also study womenswear, menswear and childrenswear and other design niches, including bridal, costumes, knitwear and different cultural / themed dress.

title: Fire Door Installation Awareness
overview: This course was developed to give an overview of how to safely install and use fire doors. There are practical aspects that cover safe installation and advice on considerations before installation. There will be various start dates throughout the year from October onwards. On a legal note, you will learn about the legislation and guidance associated with fire door assemblies and door sets. You will also learn about considerations before, during and after installing fire door assemblies and door sets. You will learn about the role of timber door assemblies and door sets and why it is important they are installed correctly. You will also be taught about how fire door assemblies and door sets work and their place in the wider passive fire protection system. Please email info@kirkleescollege.ac.uk to find out more about how to join this course.
fees_funding: This course costs £215 + £37 exam.

title: First Aid for Pets
overview: This course is an ideal platform to learn the basic aspects of first aid through a hands-on learning experience in the classroom. The course will cover various scenarios that you could encounter when performing first aid on pets. The course will cover how to manage fractures, CPR, control of bleeds, bandage techniques, handling /transporting injured animals, stings and insect bites. You will learn how to deal with poisoning and toxins and what contents are needed in a first aid kits for pets.Saturday 9:00 – 12:00

title: Floristry Level 2
overview: The course is taught by practical demonstrations, tasks and assignments. Designed to give students a broad understanding and appreciation of the floristry industry, learn how to produce designs and gain an understanding of floristry techniques and terminology as well as learning names of flowers and plants. Sessions will be on Monday evenings. Areas of study include, how to identify, check and monitor the quality of incoming cut materials, and how to identify and maintain the condition of plants. You will also learn how to construct tied floristry designs, and plan, prepare and construct a range of floral designs for sale. This course will also provide an introduction to working in the floristry industry and introduction to function decoration using floral displays. Please be aware you will be expected to provide your own flowers each week.
entry_requirements: You will need a keen interest in floristry, a passion for flowers and be prepared to commit to 100% attendance.

title: Food and Drink Maintenance Engineer Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: This apprenticeship is designed for individuals looking for a career as a Food and Drink Maintenance Engineer. As part of this apprenticeship, you will gain core industry knowledge, including key principles of quality management systems and processes, best practice maintenance approaches and techniques and fault-finding techniques. You will learn how to replace components, operate heat exchanging equipment and the principles of cutting and welding in the industry. Multi-skilled maintenance engineers need to maximise the benefits of technology. Depending on the type of product and plant in the company you work for, your role will include routine maintenance, fault finding and diagnosis, testing and commissioning. You will ensure that maintenance activities contribute to optimising food and drink production levels. On achievement of the qualification, you will have developed the required level of competence which will enable you to perform consistently, reliably and productively in your role and make a positive contribution to future employment.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths grade 4 or above.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.

career_opportunities: Typical roles would be Engineering Technician, leading to Engineering/Maintenance Manager.
assessment: You will complete assignments or direct learner responses, tests, (multiple-choice and short answer questions) and observations.
additional_information: To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable for a minimum of 30 hours a week.

title: Food and Drink Technical Operator Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Start)
overview: If you want to work behind-the-scenes of the food and drink industry, this apprenticeship is for you. During the apprenticeship, you will learn how to keep the manufacturing process running smoothly by learning how to operate a range of machinery, perform front-line diagnosis and resolution and how to perform basic maintenance on the equipment used by your employer. Within your job, you will be working as part of a wider team of process operatives, technical operators and site workers. You will gain valuable hands-on experience, developing your technical and professional skills. A big component of this apprenticeship is getting trained up in compliance, so you will learn how to conduct risk assessments, complete traceability records and implement incident management procedures. You will also be required to undertake thorough health and safety training to prepare you for working safely on site. When you have your college lessons, you will attend the Process Manufacturing Centre in Huddersfield to develop your knowledge, skills and behaviours relevant to the job role and food and drink industry.

title: Forensic and Criminal Investigations Level 3
overview: This course will teach you how to apply scientific principles to the law. Forensic scientists are given the task of collecting, preserving and analysing scientific evidence during the course of an investigation. This course focuses on the forensic aspects of biological, chemical and physical sciences alongside criminal investigations units, such as criminology. You will learn to develop practical and deductive reasoning skills. Work experience is a mandatory part of the course and will enable you to gain the skills and knowledge of how science has evolved in the workplace. Your learning will be supported through trips allowing you to explore forensic science nationally and internationally across different public and private sectors. This course combines practical and theoretical skills in criminal science, psychology and forensic analysis. Learning takes place in our fully-equipped labs that have state-of-the-art technologies. Weekly experiments may involve DNA testing, hair strand analysis or finger print dusting. Our science labs are fully fitted out to allow students to have the insight into a real Forensics lab. This course has been designed to provide a highly specialist, work-related qualification to give you the knowledge, understanding and skills that you need to prepare for employment in the vocational sector and it is suitable for those who wish to go on to higher education.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 5 GCSEs with the following grades: English and maths at 4 or above and 3 other GCSEs at 4 or above including a science subject.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Higher Education including degrees in forensic science, criminology, psychology, forensic fire investigationApprenticeship Employment
career_opportunities: A science qualification can open up a range of different opportunities. You could work in a science lab, in the police force as part of a forensics team, science research or teaching.
assessment: You will be assessed with coursework, practical assessments and examinations.

title: Functional Skills English in the Community
overview: At Kirklees College, we understand how difficult returning to college in adulthood can be. Study skills may need development, undiagnosed learning difficulties can become evident, anxiety around assessments is common, and for most adults, returning to education is not only about their own aspirations, it is also about their family’s wellbeing. That’s why at Kirklees College, we believe in a holistic approach to your education. Modules covered include presentation skills, exam and revision skills, time-management skills and help with anxiety, depression and other problems. They are user-friendly and compliment your English qualification where you will learn new skills to increase your confidence in reading, writing, and speaking. This course will help you to build your confidence and independence at home and at work. The first session of this course will be an assessment and classes will start the following week. To enrol on this course, please email ace@kirkleescollege.ac.uk This approach recognises that learning is not limited to formal training, but encompasses your entire experience, both in work and life. Throughout your course, you will meet enthusiastic, professional teachers who will be able to advise and guide you throughout your learning. You will take part in a programme that has been designed to equip learners in FE with a broad range of valuable skills needed in post-16 education. Classes take place at a number of community centres across North and South Kirklees, including Crosland Moor Community Centre and Greenwood Libary, Ravensthorpe. To find out more please email ace@kirkleescollege.ac.uk.
fees_funding: Free if you do not already hold a Level 2 or grade 4/C in GCSE, or if you have been assessed below a Level 2 or grade 4 GCSE.
assessment: Externally assessed exams.

They help develop practical maths skills and allow you to apply these to a range of realistic everyday situations. Whether you need to learn how to calculate percentages for work or balance your personal finances, Functional Skills Maths will focus on practical maths skills used in everyday life. Our courses are available at three levels (Entry Level 3, Level 1 and Level 2) and all aim to build on your current skills. Modules covered include critical thinking skills, goal setting skills, interview techniques and help with greater independence. They are user-friendly and compliment your maths qualification where you will learn new skills to increase your confidence in a wide range of knowledge, skills and understanding of mathematical methods and concepts. title: Furniture Manufacturer (Modern Upholster) Apprenticeship Level 2 (September 2026 Entry)
qualification: L2 APPRENTICESHIP STANDARD IN FURNITURE MANUFACTURER - MODERN UPHOLSTERY
overview: This apprenticeship is designed to give a wide range of skills appropriate to the different occupations in the industry. Furniture manufacturers must be dexterous, have a good work ethic and able to demonstrate their ability to work individually and as part of a team. On the modern upholsterer pathway, you will learn how to prepare and cut fabric, fit, apply and finish top covers, including fluting, pleating and buttoning. You will learn methods used to construct frames and how to make repairs to revive and polish show-wood frame. You will use machinery to produce sewn and seamed components and understand how to select materials for suspensions, including springs, pocket springs, sprung units and webbing. The apprenticeship includes mandatory and optional units. You will develop an understanding of the manufacturing process and how you fit into it, from what materials to use through to customer expectations and quality standards. You will also understand the relevant health and safety regulations and sustainability in the industry.

Once all assessments are completed, you will go through the End Point Assessment which will involve knowledge tests and observations.

To be added to our mailing list and find out if the course is to be offered to run again, please email info@kirkleescollege.ac.ukThis course is for people with some fused glass experience. It is designed to improve your understanding of the fused glass process and inspire you to create a variety of new designs. You can expect to work with frit, confetti, stringer, metals and enamels and widen your understanding of inclusions in glass. title: Games Development Level 3
overview: The UAL Diploma in Creative Media Production (Games Development) is offered at level 2 and level 3. In partnership with the University of Huddersfield, using a project-based approach, you will learn industry standard skills that allow you to create three fully playable games in each year.Using industry standard software (Unity), you will research, plan, develop, test, and showcase your games to a selection of your peers. In year one, you will create a series of game projects with each project developing specific game development skills and processes. Alongside this you will explore the different requirements for successful employment within the Games Industry.On the second year of the course, you will extend your skills to explore 3D.You will be assessed on a final project at the end of each year, after developing the skills needed. You will undertake group projects and develop essential skills that will help you to progress to the next level, ultimately working in the games development industry.This course offers a broad range of practical study, using great facilities with industry-standard equipment. You’ll complete various assessments throughout your study, resulting in a final industry-standard games showreel, and an end of year show where you’ll showcase your work professionally.

title: Games, Esports and Media Level 2
qualification: UAL Diploma in Creative Media Production
overview: Our Level 2 Diploma in Creative Media Production and Technology (Games, Esports and Media) will help you develop a range of creative digital skills. This hybrid year combines Games Development and Content Creation for Esports, and is designed to help you find your passion between these two pathways. The combined course covers a diverse range of topics, including Game Development and Pixel Art, where you will learn the fundamentals of how games are made, from initial concepts and “retro” pixel art to playable mechanics. You will also study Video Editing and Graphic Design, mastering the software used by YouTubers and streamers to create high-quality, engaging content. Whether you want to code the next indie hit, produce professional video content, or manage a live Esports tournament, this course gives you the foundation to turn a hobby into a career. You will learn valuable industry skills including game design theory, graphic and digital design, pixel art, video editing, presentation skills and event management. You will also delve into Presentation and Events, gaining the confidence to speak on camera (shoutcasting) and the organisational skills to run your own live gaming events. You will have access to our state-of-the-art facilities at Huddersfield Centre, including our Esports suite and Macs.
entry_requirements: A minimum of four GCSEs Grade 3 or above to include English and maths. If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: You can progress to Level 3 Game Development, Level 3 Esports and Content Creation, or our other Level 3 Creative Digital Courses.
career_opportunities: Job roles you can progress to include computer games tester, computer games developer, Esports organiser, web developer, software developer, amongst other careers in the digital sector.
assessment: You will be assessed through a mix of written assessments and project work.

title: Groundworker Apprenticeship Level 2 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: Groundworkers are an integral part of any site project, and are responsible for preparing sites ready for building works to take place. On this apprenticeship, you will enhance the skills you will be learning on the job with specialist training at our Brunel Construction Centre in Huddersfield. You will also learn about site health and safety, teamwork and professional conduct. As a groundworker, you will be responsible for working with a range of trades on-site, as well as site supervisors, so it is important that you learn the skills of good project management too. This apprenticeship standard will develop your knowledge and understanding of building projects as a whole. During your college hours, you will learn how to prepare surfaces and sub-surfaces for building works, including making roads and pathways, concreting, and drainage systems development. Before you start your apprenticeship, you must have found a suitable employer. The college and your employer will then work together to complete the apprenticeship sign up process and organise your timetable.
