title: Gym Instruction Certificate Level 2
overview: This course is an industry ready qualification. By completing this qualification, you will meet industry requirements to become a Gym Instructor, as set out within the CIMSPA professional standard. It is the first year of a two-year programme towards qualifying as a Level 3 personal trainer. This qualification must be achieved before moving on to study a Level 3 Personal Training qualification. The course will cover anatomy and physiology for exercise and fitness instructors, how to provide a positive customer experience in the exercise environment, lifestyle management and health awareness, gym-based programme planning and preparation and programme delivery and professional instruction. The course teaches the essential knowledge and skills required to instruct and evaluate one-to-one gym programmes to facilitate, educate and support clients in a gym or health club environment. Alongside your studies, you will gain real life experience of gym operations by supporting the staff team running the Kirklees College Waterfront Gym.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 5 GCSEs with the following grades: English at 4 or above and maths at 3 or above and 3 other GCSEs at 4 or above. Candidates need to have a basic understanding of how the human body functions and have experience using the gym.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Personal Trainer Diploma Level 3Sport and Physical Activity Introductory Certificate Level 3
career_opportunities: Gym instruction, personal trainer, gym management, leisure management and operations.
assessment: You will be assessed through a portfolio of evidence, including a combination of practical, written assignments?and multiple-choice exam.

title: Hair and Beauty Level 1
overview: This course has been designed to allow you to develop your hair, beauty and make up skills, techniques and knowledge so you can progress further in your desired role within the beauty industry. You will also be presented with the opportunity to further your own knowledge and skills by working with hand and nail care, hair, skin care, nail art and face painting. You will create a hair and beauty image using colour through make up, nail polish, temporary hair colour and accessories and design a mood board to collect your ideas. You will develop the skills and techniques that are used to create the image and present your finished design. You will be able to learn within our purpose-built hairdressing and beauty salons stocked with all the essentials to create your desired looks. Our salons are open to the public so you will be working within a professional environment from the start of your course. Students will practice on real clients and may also practice treatments on other students on the same course at Kirklees College.
entry_requirements: A minimum of GCSE English and maths grade 1 or above. It is also expected that students complete their current course of study to the best of their ability and maintain a positive approach to continuing their studies.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.

career_opportunities: This course is an ideal foundation to start a career in beauty therapy or hairdressing.

additional_information: You will need to purchase both kit and uniform to be able to do this course. These are available from the college.

title: Hair and Media Make-Up Level 2
overview: This course includes all the elements required for you to work effectively as a hair and media make-up artist within the ever-changing hair and beauty industry. You will gain skills in the art of dressing hair, photographic make-up, as well as creating an image based on current trends in the industry. Throughout the course, you will be presented with further opportunities to develop your skills in areas such as temporary hair colours, tanning, body art, themed face painting, further hair artistry, as well as the client care skills required to develop a client centred approach to your treatments. You will also gain knowledge around health and safety and effective communication which is required to work as a junior hair and make-up artist. This course is recognised by UK leading professional bodies within the hair and beauty industry. You will be able to learn within our purpose-built hairdressing and beauty salons stocked with all the essentials to create your desired looks. Our salons are open to the public, so you will be working within a professional environment from the start of your course. Students will practice on real clients and may also practice treatments on other students on the same course at Kirklees College.

title: Hair Professionals (Barbering) Apprenticeship Level 2 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.The barbering industry is rapidly expanding as interest in male grooming continues to grow. This apprenticeship will support you entering this industry. You will need to be in employment for a minimum of 30 days and will attend college one day a week. A key skill taught on this apprenticeship is learning to work with all hair types. You will need to adapt your techniques to accommodate straight hair and curly hair. You will also learn how important different shaving techniques are for different skin types. All this information is gathered via client consultations, which is another element of your apprenticeship. On your apprenticeship, you will learn all elements of barbering, starting with shampooing and conditioning hair. You will learn to cut hair using different cutting techniques and learn how to style and finish hair. As an apprentice barber, you will be expected to be able to cut facial hair into a shape and provide a shaving service for men. A good barber has excellent communication skills and understands the importance of excellent customer service and professional behaviours. You will also be taught about employee rights and responsibilities and the importance of health and safety in the both the workplace and industry. Students will practice on real clients and may also practice treatments on other students on the same course at Kirklees College.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths grade 2 or above.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Level 3 Barbering apprenticeshipLevel 3 Barbering full-time or part-time courseEmployment
assessment: All apprentices are assessed via an end point assessment. This takes place once you have completed all units of your apprenticeship. Your end point assessment will be made up of professional discussion, completion of a practical test, a workplace observation and an online multiple-choice assessment.

title: Hair Professionals (Hairdressing) Apprenticeship Level 2 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.The hairdressing industry continues to grow, with an apprenticeship a popular route to employment. Your apprenticeship will involve a minimum of 30 hours working in a salon and one day a week at college. As an apprentice hairdresser, you will be expected to develop excellent communication skills and a commitment to providing high levels of customer care. As part of your apprenticeship, you will also learn how to colour and lighten hair and the importance of using chemicals safely. Depending on your salon, you will also get the opportunity to look at perming hair, hair relaxing treatments and techniques and hair extension services. All apprentices are taught safe working practices and health and safety modules. You will learn to shampoo and condition hair. Your scissor skills will develop by cutting hair using a range of techniques. It is important that you know how to style and finish hair to create a variety of looks. Hairdressers need to accommodate all hair types ranging from straight hair to very curly, wiry hair and you will learn to adapt to your client’s hair type. By the end of your apprenticeship, you also need to be able to carry out consultations with clients and demonstrate the professionalism, values and behaviours associated with your role. You should be able to work without supervision to a high level of precision with exceptional client care skills. Students will practice on real clients and may also practice treatments on other students on the same course at Kirklees College.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths grade 2 or above.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Level 3 Hair Professionals Diploma (Hairdressing) apprenticeshipLevel 3 Hairdressing (full-time or part-time)Employment
assessment: All apprenticeships are assessed via an end point assessment. This takes place once you have completed all units of your apprenticeship. Your end point assessment will be made up of professional discussion, completion of a practical test, a work place observation and an online multiple-choice assessment.

overview: This course has been designed to introduce you to a wide range of practical skills that will allow you to begin a successful and exciting career in the hairdressing industry, including developing your visual skills when displaying retail stock. As you advance your practical skills during the course, you will also develop your knowledge of the different types of hair and the health and safety requirements to effectively run a salon, as well as how to present yourself in a professional environment. You will gain skills and knowledge about the large variety of services available within the industry, such as applying temporary colour, shampooing and conditioning and the creative skills involved in the art of dressing hair. meta_description: Are you looking to work in the hair and beauty industry? This course will provide you with a great introduction to hairdressing.

title: Hairdressing Level 1 Evening (Part Time)
overview: The course usually takes place over two evenings a week, it allows you to fit a qualification around work or family life if required. The course consists of practical assessments in a salon environment and allows you to progress your skills with continual supervision and support. You will need to attend on a Tuesday, 5-7.30pm and Thursday, 5-9pm. As part of this qualification you will gain transferrable skills, this will provide you with the opportunity to develop your own personal and social skills. Skills you will develop include communication, verbal and body, mental creativity, problem solving, social and cultural awareness. To enrol on this course, please contact info@kirkleescollege.ac.uk This course provides the essential practical skills and knowledge of the theory based within a Level 1 qualification. Practical skills you will learn include, shampooing, blow drying and styling womens and mens hair, setting, plaiting, hair up. You will learn how to provide effective advice and consultation and work as a team. This course provides an opportunity to gain valuable knowledge and basic skills to prepare you for working in the hairdressing industry, enabling you to achieve an industry recognised qualification. Alongside your level 1 you will also be preparing for level 2 progression. Cutting and colour enrichment will also be delivered as part of the course (not assessed).
entry_requirements: No formal qualifications required, however, good level of English, a passion for the industry and a creative flair is essential.
fees_funding: £1215 plus uniform and kit (approx £200) This course can be paid for in installments. For further information on this please email customerservices@kirkleescollege.ac.uk
progression: A natural progression route would be hairdressing level 2, however barbering may also be an option.
career_opportunities: You could gain employment in a salon at a junior level, or at this level you can work in a salon as an apprentice
assessment: You will be assessed continually, either by observation or project work. The certificate is awarded on completion of a portfolio of work. There is substantial theory work to be completed in your own time and written tests.

title: Hairdressing Level 2
overview: This course has been designed to teach you all the skills required to work within the hairdressing industry, as well as the promotion of products and services to your clients. This course is recognised by the UK’s leading professional body (The Hairdressing Council). During this practical course, you will get hands-on experience of shampooing, conditioning and treating the hair and scalp, using a variety of products and massage techniques. You will gain the skills to cut hair using some basic techniques, colour the hair using semi-permanent and permanent dye and style your client’s hair to complete the look. Throughout the course, you will be taught how to monitor and maintain health and safety, you will learn about client care and how to effectively communicate with your clients to meet their demand as all these skills are essential to work in the hairdressing industry. You will be able to learn within our purpose-built hairdressing and beauty salons stocked with all the essentials to create your desired looks. Our salons are open to the public, so you will be working within a professional environment from the start of your course. Students will practice on real clients and may also practice treatments on other students on the same course at Kirklees College.

overview: You will learn a wide variety of hair cuts, and how to choose and apply a range of hair colours to your client, mindful of health and safety. Alongside this main part of the course you would also study setting, styling and finishing hair and learn how to recommend suitable hair products to help your client maintain their look. Students joining this course will be expected to have a uniform and kit. These can be purchased from the college.

title: Health and Social Care (BTEC) Level 2
overview: This course enables you to gain an understanding of the health and social care sector and provides you with an introduction to the key areas relating to current practice within the industry. You will have the opportunity to explore how humans grow and develop throughout their lives and investigate the factors that affect this growth and development. You will begin to gain an understanding of the variety of social factors that can influence a person’s health and wellbeing through studying the impacts of nutrition, different mental and physical disorders, as well as creative and therapeutic activities. You will take part in 60+ hours of work placement in order to support the theory you are learning. Placement supports learning by providing opportunities for you to apply knowledge to practice, receive feedback and reflect on your own experience.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 4 GCSEs with the following grades: English at 3 or above and maths at 2 or above and 2 other GCSEs at 3 or above. A DBS check is also required.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.

assessment: You will be assessed through written assignments, exams, practical tasks, group work, presentations and reports, which will be discussed with you before you start.

title: Heavy Vehicle Service and Maintenance Technician Apprenticeship Level 3 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.A heavy vehicle service (HV) technician inspects and repairs HVs either at a dealership that focuses on a particular manufacturer, or for an independent garage or large fleet operator dealing with different makes of vehicles. This apprenticeship covers replacing simple parts and solving complex faults to using diagnostic methods and equipment. Day-to-day tasks faced by the technician are constantly changing, driven by the introduction of more complex technologies and diagnostic techniques. This apprenticeship will teach you the knowledge and understanding required to work with heavy Vehicles. This includes: diagnostic and logical problem-solving techniques, health and safety knowledge, environmental awareness, operator’s “O” Licence requirements, how to service, inspect and maintain vehicles and trailers to the expected standards, customer expectations and implications of work carried out and the requirements of providing roadside assistance. Today’s technician has to demonstrate technical expertise in their role. You must have strong problem-solving skills and knowledge of the theoretical and practical aspects of vehicle systems and associated ancillaries. Technicians should be able to work independently and operate as an effective team member. They should also have good customer handling skills and be able to identify ways in which they can work more efficiently. As a motor vehicle apprentice, you will be studying at our college’s specialist Engineering Centre which boasts state-of-the-art equipment and workshops. You will find all the relevant workshops and demonstration areas required for you to complete your studies with Kirklees College.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths grade 4 or above.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Level 4 and work towards becoming an Automotive Master TechnicianEmployment
career_opportunities: Heavy Vehicle (HV) technician
assessment: You will be assessed through annual gateway assessments and a final end of programme assessment.

title: Higher National Certicate in Space Technologies for England HTQ (Part Time)
overview: This Level 4 HNC in Space Technologies provides a broad foundation in space engineering principles and satellite technologies. The programme develops scientific, analytical and computational skills required for entry-level roles within the growing UK space sector. You will study Engineering Design, Engineering Maths, Engineering Science, Managing a Professional Engineering Project, Programming for Engineers, CAD/CAM, Space Environment and Applications, and Space Technologies and Manufacturing. You will gain understanding of spacecraft systems, satellite manufacturing processes and the impact of the space environment on engineering design. The qualification supports progression to the Level 5 HND or entry-level technical roles in space and aerospace industries. This is a really exciting and innovative qualification and is delivered at our state-of-the-art Engineering Centre in Huddersfield. You will be taught by experts in the industry.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 48 UCAS Tariff points from:BTEC National (Diploma or Extended Diploma) in a relevant subjectAccess to HE DiplomaT Levels, A LevelsScottish Higher/Advanced Higher with at least one subject at Advanced HigherAppropriate Level 3 qualificationPlusGCSE English – Grade C/4 or aboveGCSE Maths – Grade C/4 or above*For mature applicants, some relevant industrial background may be taken in lieu of A Levels/BTECs
fees_funding: This course costs £8,000. You can apply for a student loan through Student Finance England.
progression: The course supports progression to the Level 5 HND in Space Technologies.
career_opportunities: This course prepares you for entry-level roles such as Space Engineering Technician, Satellite Manufacturing Technician or Ground Station Technician within the growing UK space sector.
assessment: You will be assessed mainly through assignments, set both internally and by the awarding body.

qualification: Pearson BTEC Level 5 Higher National Diploma in Space Technologies for England (HTQ)
overview: The Level 5 HND in Space Technologies develops advanced knowledge of spacecraft systems, mission design and satellite communication technologies. The programme prepares you for specialist technical roles and further academic progression within the space engineering sector. You will complete a range of modules including Further Mathematics, Professional Engineering Management, Research Project, Manufacturing Systems Engineering, Sensors and Automation, Space Communications and Space Mission Design. You will integrate systems engineering, automation and communication technologies to design and evaluate complex space missions. The qualification supports progression to aerospace or space engineering degree top-up programmes and advanced technical careers. You will study at our state-of-the-art Engineering Centre in Huddersfield, where you will have access to the best equipment in the sector, learn from experts, and get ample support during your time with us.

title: HNC Digital Technologies Level 4
qualification: Higher National Certificate (HNC) / Higher Technical Qualification
overview: Digital technologies are devices that generate, store or process data and they are essential to the running of businesses in the modern world. This Higher National Certificate has been categorised as a Higher Technical Qualification, meaning that it has been designed in collaboration with a range of employers to meet the specific skills needs of the current workplace. Throughout the HNC’s modules, you will further your knowledge of a range of digital technologies and their business functions. You will also undertake modules that focus on your professional development, such as Professional Practice in the Digital Economy and Management in the Digital Economy. *This course will run subject to validation. During the course, you will be introduced to a range of digital technologies and systems, and understand how these technologies help support business operations. Modules will cover technical aspect of digital technologies and their uses, such as cyber security, programming, big data and visualisation, and web design and development.

progression: Thic course is designed to prepare you for the HND Level 5.
career_opportunities: Completion of the HNC will prepare you for a range of careers such as software development, data analysis and cyber security.
assessment: You will be assessed on a range of written and practical assignments throughout the HNC.

title: HNC Leadership and Management
qualification: Higher National Certificate (HNC) / Higher Technical Qualification (HTQ)
overview: Over the course of the HNC, you will develop your understanding of a broad range of business functions. You will tie this knowledge to practical skills for the work place through research, independent study and workplace scenarios. The HNC has been designed to meet the needs of employers and each unit you undertake will be tied to a specific leadership and management role, for example Department Manager. Each year, the awarding body Pearson will also set a unit that is based on a theme (e.g. Corporate Social Responsibility), and you will complete an assessed project around how to successfully implement and evaluate the theme within a business structure. This will develop your analytical business skills and equip you with experience of evaluating and implementing strategy within a business. You will complete eight core modules during the course. These are: The Contemporary Business Environment; Marketing Processes and Planning; Management of Human Resources; Leadership and Management; Accounting Principles; Managing a Successful Business Project; Operational Planning and Management, and Digital Business in Practice. As well as your business knowledge, you will also develop a range of employability skills. Throughout classes, assessment and individual study, you will develop a range of transferable skills including problem solving, creativity, communication, team work, leadership, digital literacy and cultural awareness.

career_opportunities: Completion of the HNC will prepare you for a range of Leadership and Management roles, including Department Manager and Team Leader.
assessment: You will be assessed on a range of written and practical assignments throughout the HNC.

title: HND in Manufacturing Engineering for England HTQ (Part Time)
qualification: Pearson BTEC Level 5 Higher National Diploma  in Manufacturing Engineering for England (HTQ)
overview: The Level 5 HND in Manufacturing Engineering develops advanced technical expertise and leadership capability for careers in production, systems engineering and industrial management. The programme builds on Level 4 knowledge and focuses on optimisation, automation and strategic engineering improvement. You will enhance your ability to evaluate manufacturing performance, manage engineering teams and implement continuous improvement strategies. The qualification supports progression to BEng (Hons) top-up degrees or professional engineering roles within advanced manufacturing sectors. You will complete a range of modules, including: Professional Engineering Management, Further Mathematics, Manufacturing Systems Engineering, Lean Manufacturing, Advanced Manufacturing Technology, Sustainability and Industrial Systems. As well as excellent pastoral and study support from a dedicated delivery team, the course also focuses much of its delivery on sustainable and renewable engineering. You will be based at our state-of-the-art Engineering Centre in Huddersfield, where you will have access to cutting-edge engineering technology and equipment.
entry_requirements: HNC in General Engineering at Level 4 and GCSE English, maths and science grade 4 (C) or above.
fees_funding: The cost for our HNC in total is £8000 over two years.
progression: Further study or employment in areas such as electrical or communication design, manufacture, maintenance or technical services in the engineering industry.
career_opportunities: Mechanical Engineer, Civil Engineer, Analyst, Electrical Engineer, Maintenance Engineer, Technical Officer.
assessment: You will be assessed by a mixture of practical skills-based tests, workplace projects, assignments and online exams.
qualification_awarding_body: Pearson BTEC Level 5 Higher National Diploma in Manufacturing Engineering for England (HTQ)

title: Hospitality Team Member Apprenticeship Level 2 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.The role of a hospitality team member is varied and although you may specialise in an area, you must be adaptable and ready to support your team members across the business during busy periods. This apprenticeship involves one day a week in college, usually a Wednesday or Thursday, working with our specialist front of house staff. As part of your apprenticeship, you will learn about all the areas associated with the hospitality, food and beverage service, including learning barista and food production. This apprenticeship supports you in developing your hospitality skills. It will help you learn to recognise your customers’ needs to improve their experience. A key skill you will learn is ensuring that your customers feel welcomed and looked after. This apprenticeship also teaches you about conference and events organisation, reservations and housekeeping. All apprentices also get the opportunity to find out about beverage services, including wine service, beer and cask ale, cocktails and mixology.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths grade 3 or above.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Employment
assessment: The end point assessment involves a culinary challenge, workplace assessment, online multiple-choice assessment and a professional discussion. You must have already passed Functional Skills Level 1 English and maths and be working towards level 2 as part of your apprenticeship.

title: Human Resources Support Apprenticeship Level 3 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.This Level 3 apprenticeship course is available to all HR professionals who wish to gain a formal qualification in HR. HR Support includes a range of knowledge skills and behaviours and these elements are assessed throughout the programme finishing in an end point assessment and professional discussion. The course programme includes employment law, organisational structures, HR tools, business understanding, policies and HR function. Upon successful completion of the course, you can apply for membership of the Chartered Institute of Professional Development. You will gain robust technical knowledge of Human Resources systems, processes, procedures and legalities. Classroom sessions are delivered by our industry experienced tutors. You will also be supported through progress reviews in the workplace by the assigned Trainer Assessor.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths at grade 4.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: HR qualification at Level 4Professional HR qualification (CIPD)Employment
career_opportunities: Upon successful completion of the course, you can apply for membership of the Chartered Institute of Professional Development.
assessment: During the course of the apprenticeship, you will be assessed using witness testimonies, project work, written reports and assessment, reviews and online portfolio. The final end point assessment completes the apprenticeship with professional discussion and a project.

title: Improving Personal Exercise, Health and Nutrition
qualification: NCFE Level 2 Certificate in Improving Personal Exercise, Health and Nutrition.
overview: This course is suitable for anyone who wants to improve their knowledge and understanding of health, nutrition and fitness. You will complete three mandatory modules around well-being and exercise, understanding the role of nutrition in exercise and how to plan and prepare for personal exercise and nutrition. This course is taught completely online and can be completed in your own time. We advise that the course takes around 8 weeks to complete the core modules that make up the qualification.Before starting the course, you must complete a pre-assessment. This needs to be submitted at least two weeks prior to the enrolment start date to confirm your place on the course.This course will have starts in October, February and April.
entry_requirements: There are no formal entry requirements for this course. However, we recommend that learners have an intermediate level of reading and writing in English. As the course is delivered online, basic digital skills will also be needed. Before starting the course, you will be asked to complete a pre-assessment. This needs to be submitted at least two weeks prior to the enrolment start date to confirm your place on the course.
fees_funding: This course costs £400. To see if you are eligible for any funding, please e-mail customerservices@kirkleescollege.ac.uk
qualification_awarding_body: NCFE Level 2 Certificate in Improving Personal Exercise, Health and Nutrition.

title: Information Communications Technician Apprenticeship Level 3 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.On this apprenticeship, you will gain the knowledge and skills needed to efficiently operate and control IT and telecommunications infrastructure. You will be trained to interpret technical specifications, apply security policies and use infrastructure management tools to automate the provisioning and monitoring of IT systems and services. This includes understanding virtual hardware, software, network services, data storage, and cloud computing. Additionally, you will gain proficiency in resolving hardware and software faults, both remotely and in-person. You will focus on maximising productivity and user experience by monitoring and maintaining systems and platforms. You will be equipped to contribute to the preparation of new or changed services, maintain regulatory and professional standards and monitor system performance in relation to business needs, security and sustainability. You will develop the ability to provide technical support to both internal and external customers. You will learn to diagnose IT problems, troubleshoot routine and non-routine issues and use various communication channels to assist stakeholders. This apprenticeship will help you prepare for a range of specialist roles in the IT sector. You will be equipped to work in various organisations, large or small, across different sectors, including public, private, and voluntary organisations. The training will cover networking, server management, IT essentials, secure communications, programming and databases, among other essential tasks and projects.
entry_requirements: GCSE grade 4 or above in GCSE English, maths and an IT qualification or relevant experience. To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Higher Education qualification in Computing and IT Full-time employment
assessment: You will need to complete a portfolio of evidence, project report with questioning and end-point assessment.

raw_accordion.entry_requirements: GCSE grade 4 or above in GCSE English, maths and an IT qualification or relevant experience. To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
raw_accordion.course_progression: Higher Education qualification in Computing and IT Full-time employment
raw_accordion.assessment_methods: You will need to complete a portfolio of evidence, project report with questioning and end-point assessment.

title: Intermediate Ceramics
overview: This course has already started but may run again after Easter. To be added to our mailing list and find out if the course is running, please email info@kirkleescollege.ac.ukOur Intermediate Ceramics Hand Building course is designed for learners who have completed our beginner programme or who already have a grounding in the core hand-building methods: coiling, slab-building and pinch pot techniques. Building on these foundations, this course will guide you through one or two more ambitious projects, giving you the confidence to create larger, more refined ceramic pieces. All materials and tools are provided. Fired work is charged per finished piece to cover kiln costs—for example, a lamp base would cost £10 in total for both the bisque and glaze firings. Throughout the course, you will deepen your understanding of design, surface decoration and glazing. You’ll experiment with a range of decorative techniques and develop your own creative approach while working at a comfortable, supportive pace. This friendly, structured course is ideal for those looking to take their ceramic skills to the next level and create unique, professionally finished work.Class times: Fridays Group 1 Morning: 9:00-12:00Group 2 Afternoon: 13:00-16:00
fees_funding: The cost is of this course is £85. To find out if you are eligible for funding, please email customerservices@kirkleescollege.ac.uk

title: Introduction To Culinary Skills Level 1
overview: This course will provide you with practical skills in food preparation, cooking and food service. Your lessons will take place in our purpose-built kitchens and are taught by chef lecturers. This kitchen-based course is hands-on and will give you a real taste of working in the catering industry. You will learn how to use the different types of industry standard equipment that are provided at the college. A key element of catering is understanding about health and safety in a kitchen and food safety, both of which are built into this course. Your understanding of creating healthy and special diets will also be developed. You will develop your basic kitchen skills. This involves learning basic knife skills to then support learning different cooking techniques for meat, fish, vegetables and desserts. The skills you will learn include how to boil, poach and steam, stewing and braising, roasting and grilling and frying, as well as spending time in the bakery working on baking skills. Students will also have an opportunity to visit different catering establishments and have guest speakers attend class. This has previously included a kitchen takeover by the Royal Navy and visits from professional chefs. All students are encouraged to take part in our in-house competition Hell’s Kitchen.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 4 GCSEs with the following grades: English at 2 or above and maths at 1 or above and 2 other GCSEs at 2 or above.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Professional Cookery NVQ Level 2Professional Patisserie, Confectionary and Cafe Culture Level 2Apprenticeship
career_opportunities: There are a variety of careers available in the hospitality and catering industry. The skills learnt on this course will equip you to work in a range of kitchens as a junior chef.
assessment: You will be assessed through written work, presentations and practical assessments of your skills.

title: Introduction to Digital Graphics for Communication and Digital Marketing
overview: Over nine weeks, you will learn how to bridge the gap between “having an idea” and “executing a design,” learning to navigate the industry-standard power of Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator. This course is built around practical, real-world business applications. Rather than focusing on abstract art, we dive straight into the workflows that matter: editing corporate photography, designing scalable logos and icons, and building high-impact social media graphics. Key learning outcomes of this course include precision editing, where you will master non-destructive photo retouching and background removal; vector versatility, creating scalable brand assets and typography that never lose quality. You will also learn technical literacy associated with design, including understanding file formats and colour profiles. Finally, you will also learn workflow efficiency skills, learning the shortcuts used by pros to produce high-quality graphics. This course will run on Thursdays, 2pm-4pm You will master the distinction between raster and vector environments, ensuring your designs are always crisp, professional, and technically correct, whether it’s destined for a mobile screen or a large-scale print banner. By the end of the course, you will possess a versatile “Digital Toolkit” that allows you to maintain brand consistency, shorten project turnaround times, and produce polished marketing collateral that resonates with your target audience. This is more than a software tutorial; it is a strategic upskilling programme designed to turn internal teams into agile, visual storytellers. There will be an end of course assessment and on completion of this you will be issued with a Kirklees College Certificate.

title: Introduction to Hair and Beauty Therapy
overview: Introduction to the Hair and Beauty IndustryThis unit introduces learners to the structure of the hair and beauty industry and the different roles found in a salon. Students develop an understanding of professional behaviour, personal presentation, and good communication when interacting with clients and colleagues. The unit also highlights the importance of teamwork, organisation, and maintaining a professional attitude in a working salon environment Plaiting & Twisting In this unit, learners practise basic hair styling techniques using plaiting and twisting methods. Students learn how to prepare the hair, section it correctly, and create simple styles while following safe and hygienic working practices. The unit helps learners improve coordination, patience, and attention to detail while developing confidence when carrying out practical hair styling tasks.This course will run on Wednesdays, 11:30-2:30 Providing Basic ManicureThis unit focuses on developing practical nail care skills while following professional hygiene and safety procedures. Learners practise preparing the work area, carrying out a basic manicure, and presenting a neat finish. Students also develop an understanding of client care by communicating politely and ensuring the client feels comfortable during the service. Developing Professional and Workplace SkillsLearners develop transferable skills that are important for working in the hair and beauty sector. These include time management, organisation, and working effectively with others. Students also learn about health and safety responsibilities, maintaining a clean workspace, and providing good client care to ensure clients feel safe, comfortable, and well looked after in a salon environment.

title: Introduction to Hot Stone Therapy
overview: This course is an introduction to hot stone therapy, including the history, benefits, and basic techniques. You will learn how to prepare stones, manage temperature safely, and perform basic massage strokes to enhance relaxation and wellbeing. You will practise combining stone placement and hand massage to deliver an effective treatment while receiving feedback to refine your skills.This course will run Fridays, 12:15-3:30pm. You will cover essential health and safety practices, client consultation procedures, and hygiene standards. You will understand how to carry out thorough client assessments, maintain a safe treatment environment, and manage any risks associated with hot stone massage. You will also learn about professional client care, communication, and post-treatment advice. You will learn how to provide a positive client experience, address individual needs, and ensure client comfort and satisfaction throughout the session.

title: Introduction to Uniformed Services Level 1
overview: Life in the public services can be challenging, rewarding, interesting and exciting. This one-year course has been designed to be a stepping stone into a fulfilling and rewarding career within the public and uniformed services industry. You will develop your knowledge of how the industry works and gain practical skills, such as map reading, self-management, team work and development of your own personal skills. You will be put to the test through the development of your own fitness levels to match those within the industry. You will be required to show commitment and willingness to learn during the course. Through both practical and theory-based lessons, you will gain an understanding of what is required within the many roles available in the uniformed services. We will help you to develop your organisation and leadership skills. You will then have the opportunity to learn outside of the classroom by partaking in a number of social action projects.
entry_requirements: A minimum of four GCSEs with the following grades: English at 2 or above and maths at 1 or above and two other GCSEs at 2 or above.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us
progression: Level 2 Physical Preparation for Entry to the Uniformed Protective Services
career_opportunities: This course is a perfect stepping stone support a career in the public services sector, which offers jobs in the police, health service, fire service and armed forces.
assessment: There are no formal exams throughout this course. You will be assessed to nationally set standards through various activities such as discussions, group presentations and activities, written assignments, practical demonstrations, fitness tests and observations.
additional_information: Students will be required to have a Kirklees College UPS uniform to participate on this course. This can be purchased from gbkits.co.ukIf you are worried about the cost of this kit then students can apply for support via the student bursary scheme.

title: IPET Level 3 Certificate in Dog Grooming and Introduction to Styling
overview: The qualification is designed for people who wish to gain the skills and knowledge to prepare, style and finish dog grooming treatments on a selection of dog breeds. You will also learn about health and safety in a dog grooming salon and gain an understanding of heath checking, handling and canine behaviour. The qualification is a combination of practical and theory teaching. Assessment is via developmental portfolio of evidence covering at least 10 different breeds and four different coat types. Units included within this qualification are: Health and Safety in a Dog Grooming Environment; Promote the Welfare of Dogs conforming with Relevant Legislation; Assess and Plan Dog Grooming Styles and Maintenance of Equipment; Health Checking, Handling and Canine Behaviour, and Prepare, Style and Finish a Selection of Dog Breeds.

title: IT Skills for Beginners
overview: Computers can be intimidating, especially if you didn’t grow up with them. Some tasks that seem simple to experienced users can seem impossible to new users. In this free, friendly course you’ll learn more about the types of computers and operating systems in use today. You’ll also gain a better understanding of the basic parts of a computer, how applications are used, connecting to the Internet, and much more. Classes will run on Thursdays, 10:00am – 12:30pmThe first session of this course will be an assessment and classes will start the following week. To join this course, email ace@kirkleescollege.ac.uk This computer basics course goes at your own pace to make sure you understand the basics. On the course you will learn buttons and ports on a Computer and what the basic parts do. You will learn about Microsoft office and the internet and how to keep yourself safe online. At the end of the course you will have the confidence to try new things and move on to our next digital level.
fees_funding: Free if you do not already hold a Level 1 in Digital Skills or have been assessed at Level 1 or below.

title: KC Supported Internship
overview: KC Supported Internship is a one-year supported internship programme. It is a transition to work initiative for students aged 18-24 with a learning disability. It is targeted at students whose goal is competitive employmentwithin the local community. Kirklees College has partnered with local employers to facilitate this course. Throughout the year, you will have three work placements to help you build the skills required to gain and maintain employment in your chosen sector. Placements are matched to your career aspirations, skills and personal development goals. Your tutor and Job Coach will work alongside you to support you in your development towards meaningful employment. The aim of a Supported Internship is to support you in your first steps towards gaining meaningful employment. You will spend one day a week learning an employability curriculum, developing vital employability skills, such as communication, team work, self-advocacy and technology skills. As well as CV writing and interview preparation. You will also spend three days a week on work placement. Upon successful completion of the course you can continue to receive support through REAL Employment. Past students have gone to work in NHS, Nando’s and other various roles in the community.
entry_requirements: There are no formal entry requirements. You will take part in an interview to see if the course is right for you and to see if you have any additional support needs.Students will need to have SEND to apply, please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: This course will help you secure employment of 16 hours or more a week.
career_opportunities: This course is designed for students on an employability pathway. Students will be supported to apply for employment during the course of their studies and receive ongoing support from REAL Employment.
assessment: You will attend regular progress and development reviews with your tutor, family, job coach, rotation manager and mentor.

title: Laboratory Technician Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: As an apprentice laboratory technician, you will attend college one day a week and spend the rest of the week in a company. A laboratory technician may carry out both routine and one-off laboratory testing and perform a variety of technical support functions across the organisation. On this apprenticeship you will need to work safely in a laboratory and meet the requirements of quality standards whilst demonstrating technical competence in the use of instrumentation and equipment. You will be required to produce reliable, accurate data and analyse, interpret and evaluate data. Laboratory technicians work in a wide range of organisations, including but not exclusively, chemical, primary and secondary pharmaceutical, biotechnology, formulated products, nuclear companies and analytical science services. You will prepare for laboratory tasks using the appropriate scientific techniques, procedures and methods as well as performing laboratory tasks following specified methodologies. You will be expected to communicate and interact effectively within a team using a full range of skills. You will learn the importance of working independently and taking responsibility for initiating and completing tasks and understanding the impact of your work on others.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 5 GCSEs with the following grades: English and maths at 4 or above and 3 other GCSEs at 4 or above including a science subject.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable
progression: Employment in the science industryHigher level apprenticeship
career_opportunities: You could find employment in a Supervisory/senior technician roles with additional qualifications, potential routes to scientist, technologist or educationalist.
assessment: You will be assessed both at college and in the workplace through coursework, practical assignments and examinations
additional_information: To be an apprentice you need to have suitable employment for a minimum of 30 hours a week.

title: Lead Internal Quality Assurance (IQA) (February 2026 entry)
qualification: TQUK Level 4 Lead Internal Quality Assurance (IQA)
overview: On this course you will learn a arange of techniques to help you lead on internal quality assurance processes and practices in education. During the first module, you will produce a work plan for your own area of responsibility and explain the context in which work is to be undertaken. You will also cover ways to monitor the progress and quality of work in own area of responsibility and provide feedback. This includes monitoring and evaluating progress against agreed standards, and providing feedback to team members. You will also learn to be able to allocate and agree responsibilities with team members and provide SMART targets (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic and Time-bound). Building on work plans, you will also gain the skills to be able to review and amend plans of work for own area of responsibility and communicate changes. Review and amend work plan where changes are needed.There are four weeks of face-to-face sessions for this course, but you will have a year to complete the qualification. Lessons starting on 5th March take place on Thursdays, 4 – 7pm.
fees_funding: This course costs £811.

title: Level 1 Diploma in Groundworks (Construction and Civil Engineering)
qualification: NOCN Level 1 Diploma in Construction and Civil Engineering Services
overview: This is an excellent course if you want to start a career in groundworks, as it will equip you with the relevant knowledge and skills to prepare you for a career in Construction or Civil Engineering. You will also undertake two optional modules, lay modular paving and lay and finish concrete, to develop your understanding of groundworks. This qualification is made up of a core set of mandatory units that will support your practical skills development, and underpin your associated knowledge. The mandotory units are: health, safety and welfare in construction and associated industries; knowledge of technical information, quantities and communication with others; knowledge of construction technologies, and cut construction related materials.

progression: You can progress to further training at Level 2 and on successfully completing a Level 2 qualification, you can progress into a job role in the workplace, which could be in Construction or Civil Engineering.

title: Level 1 Diploma in Progression
overview: Qualifications in Progression are a flexible and responsive range of general and pre-vocational qualifications devised with the aim to progress learners into further learning and/or employment. Each student will complete the programme alongside their Maths and English GCSEs. This course is a great option for anyone looking to gain a new qualification and get a fresh start after a long time out of education or employment. You will also work on your understanding on people and relationships, including family relationships, developing confidence and self-awareness, and understanding stress and stress management techniques. You will also study alcohol awareness, healthy living and develop your personal learning skills. Sessions will be taught by a range of subject specialists who can guide and support you throughout the course. You will cover a diverse range of topics, starting with a focus on society and sociology, including local history, understanding diversity in society, and young people and law and order. Your citizenship learning will also cover prejudice and discrimination. This course also has a strong emphasis on skills for employment, and you will focus on your career planning, CV writing and job skills as part of the programme.
entry_requirements: A minimum of three GCSEs with the following grades: English and maths at grade E/2 or above and 1 other GCSEs at grade E/2 or above. Applicants must be 19+.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
fees_funding: The cost of this courses is £1750Please contact customerservices@kirkleescollege.ac.uk for further information regarding fees for this course and how to pay them.
progression: Progression to Access HE Level 2Access to HE Level 3 courses (dependent on achievement of GCSE English and/or maths) Other college courses, apprenticeships and employment
career_opportunities: Career pathways for those progressing onto Higher Education include Nursing, Midwifery, Operating Departmental Practice, Radiography, Clinical Sciences (Health Science Professions pathway), Social Practice, Criminology, Teaching, Law, Business, Probation, History, English (Social Sciences/Humanities pathways). For students not planning to progress to HE, this course equips students with range of employability skills and behaviours suitable for any workplace.
assessment: You will be assessed through a mix of written assignments, presentations and group work.

title: Light Vehicle and Maintenance and Repair Principles Diploma Level 2
overview: This course is designed to support you develop a career in the motor industry, whether you want to maintain and repair different types of vehicles (cars, vans, trucks, or even motorcycles), fit and replace parts and tyres, run your own car valeting business or work on hybrid vehicles safely. You do not need any previous experience or need to be in a current work placement. Under the guidance of our expert tutors who have worked in the industry themselves, you will have access to state-of-the-art equipment in our workshops. You will also have the opportunity to complete a work placement to give you experience of working in the motor vehicle industry. You will have the opportunity to cover a wide range of skills and knowledge by taking a combination of mandatory and optional units. You will develop a basic understanding of health and safety, engines, cooling and lubrication, fuel, transmission systems, wheels and tyres, brakes, vehicle inspection and steering and suspension.

meta_description: This course will suit you if you have already developed a good foundation of skills and knowledge required to become a vehicle service technician and are ready to move to the next level.

title: Light Vehicle Maintenance and Repair Principles Level 3
overview: This course is suitable if you are interested in developing a career in the motor industry, whether you want to maintain and repair different types of vehicles (cars, vans, trucks, or even motorcycles), fit and replace parts and tyres, run your own car valeting business or work on hybrid vehicles safely. You will learn how to fault find using diagnostic equipment, standard servicing, repairs and maintenance and fit and service accessories like stereos and alarms. You will be able to road test vehicles to check repairs and be competent in telling customers what repairs are needed and estimated time and cost for these. This course is suitable if you would like to develop practical skills and broaden your understanding of motor vehicle systems and more generally in developing others in the industry. You must already be working with minimum supervision and have a good record of safety, fault correction and ensuring work meets the required standard.? Under the guidance of our expert tutors who have worked in the industry themselves, you will have access to state-of-the-art equipment in our purpose-built workshops. You will also have the opportunity to complete a work placement to give you experience of working in the motor vehicle industry.

title: Light Vehicle Service and Maintenance Technician Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: A motor vehicle service and maintenance Technician servicing and repairing light vehicles, such as cars and vans, either works in dealerships, focusing on particular manufacturers or in an independent garage dealing with many different makes of vehicles. This apprenticeship will teach you to work independently and also operate as an effective team member and have good customer handling skills. You will also understand how workshops function. As a motor vehicle apprentice, you will be studying at our college’s specialist Engineering Centre which boasts state-of-the-art equipment and workshops. You will find all the relevant workshops and demonstration areas required for you to complete your studies with Kirklees College. Technicians working in large dealerships often work with other departments, for example carrying out work for the sales department and ordering parts from the parts department, whereas apprentices in smaller independent garages may be called upon to carry out some of the function of the other departments themselves, for example managing their own delivery of parts. The apprenticeship will teach you how to work on all the systems found within the vehicle. The day-to-day work ranges from replacing simple parts through to solving complex faults with the use of diagnostic methods and equipment. The tasks faced are constantly changing, driven by the introduction of more complex technologies and diagnostic techniques.

title: Machining Technician Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: To be an apprentice you need to be employed for a minimum of 30 hours a week and attend college one day a week. With this apprenticeship, you will also complete a block placement with college at the start of your apprenticeship. Machinists in the advanced manufacturing engineering sector are predominantly involved in highly skilled, complex and precision work. Throughout your apprenticeship, you will carry out complex fault diagnosis and repair activities applicable to the tool, die, jig and fixture environment. This will include maintaining mechanical equipment, fluid and pneumatic power equipment, electrical and electronic equipment and process control equipment. You will also undertake testing to confirm correct operation, and of the effectiveness of repairs and maintenance activities carried out. As an apprentice, you will be taught to understand mathematical techniques, formula and calculations in a toolmaking environment. You will learn the structure, properties and characteristics of common materials used for the manufacture and repair of tooling, moulds, dies and jigs and fixtures, as well as how to set up and operate machinery and equipment effectively. You will learn how the relevant tools, dies, jigs and fixtures being manufactured/maintained function. Technicians must comply with applicable legislation and organisational safety requirements and be expected to work both individually and as part of a manufacturing team, working with minimum supervision and taking responsibility for the quality and accuracy of the work they undertake. You will also learn to read and interpret relevant data and documentation used to produce and/or maintain tool and die components, assemblies and systems.
entry_requirements: A minimum of Grade 4 or above in GCSE English, maths and a science or technology subject.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Higher level apprenticeship/HNCDegree level qualificationFull-time employment
career_opportunities: Careers in the engineering industry are varied. A qualification in engineering can open up many employment opportunities both in the UK and abroad with good earning opportunities.
assessment: You will be assessed by a combination of course work and assignments, practical work and exams. The end point assessment will involve a portfolio based occupational competence interview and professional assessment using a performance indicator report form.
additional_information: Apprentices are expected to own overalls, safety goggles and safety boots.

title: Makeup Artistry Level 3
overview: This course has been designed to teach you all the skills required to work in the beauty industry in various roles, including theatre, retail or as a self-employed make-up artist, as well as the promotion of products and services to your clients. You will learn through practical based sessions how to apply make-up, design images to fit with the current trends in the fashion and photographic industries, as well as more detailed skills, such as applying airbrush make-up to the face, camouflage make-up and designing and applying body paint. Throughout the course, you will be taught how to monitor and maintain health and safety practice within the salon. You will also learn about client care and client focused practice in salons, as well as the effective communication required to work in the beauty industry. You will be able to learn within our purpose- built hairdressing and beauty salons stocked with all the essentials to create your desired looks. Our salons are open to the public, so you will be working within a professional environment from the start of your course.

title: Media Makeup Level 3
overview: Want to combine your love for makeup with a career in the creative industries? If you’ve always dreamed of creating looks for film, TV, theatre or the fashion industry, this Level 3 VTCT Media Makeup course is for you! You will benefit from opportunities to engage with employers and professionals throughout your course, including expert guidance from industry-experienced tutors. In the classroom, learning will be hands-on and practical, and you will also learn about professional conduct and salon health and safety to prepare you for all aspects of life in industry. Taught by experts at our Pioneer Higher Skills Centre in Dewsbury, you will benefit from access to all the very best facilities and equipment in our bespoke beauty therapy studio. You will cover a range of media makeup skills, including photographic make-up, airbrush makeup, cosmetic camouflage and false eyelash application. This course will open up a range of opportunities for you, and you will leave college as a qualified makeup professional.

title: Metal Fabricator Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: The broad purpose of a fabricator and welder is to carry out metal fabrication work using equipment such as rolled steel joists, columns, channels, steel plate and metal sheet etc. You will learn how to mark out using hand tools, drilling, forming, folding and rolling. You will interact with planners, supervisors, inspectors, designers, welders, fitters, riggers and many others involved in manufacturing, production, maintenance and repair. Fabrication or welding work can include manufacturing of bridges, ships aircraft, machinery parts, cranes and anything that can be fabricated out of metal. Fabricators can work alone or in teams, in factories or on operational sites. They are responsible for the quality and accuracy of their work. This apprenticeship is ideal if you are looking for a career in the fabrication or welding environment whilst producing various fabricated components. Fabrication is creating a metal product from beginning to end, it involves everything from design to finishing. Welding is simply one action carried out during the fabrication procedure and is the process of joining two pieces of metal together with heat using a range of welding processes. As a Fabrication or Welding apprentice, you will be studying at our college’s specialist Engineering Centre which boasts state-of-the-art equipment and workshops. You will find all the relevant workshops and demonstration areas required for you to complete your studies.

assessment: You will be assessed both at college and in the workplace.

title: Mobile Air Conditioning Unit - Handling Refrigerants
qualification: L3U MOBILE AIR CONDITIONING UNIT 001 - HANDLING REFRIGERANTS
overview: Our comprehensive course is designed to provide technicians with the knowledge and skills necessary to navigate the complexities of mobile air conditioning systems and ensure compliance with relevant regulations. Whether you’re new to the field or seeking to upgrade your existing skills, this course will equip you with the expertise needed to excel in your profession. You will be able to confidently explain and use the tools, equipment and materials needed for system recovery, vacuum, recharging, flushing (if applicable), and leak detection for mobile air conditioning systems and know the procedures for handling refrigerant and minimising refrigerant losses. You will learn to interpret legislative and organisational requirements and procedures relevant to your workplace practices whilst handling refrigerants. You will understand the operating principles and functional requirements of mobile air conditioning systems and components and be able to compare the different types of refrigerants. You will learn how to work safely and act in accordance with legislation and operating procedures. You will gain the ability to carry out at least two methods of leak detection, be able to correctly store and transfer waste material and complete records accurately.

title: Motor Vehicle Entry 3
qualification: Entry Level 3 Certificate for the Introduction to Motor Vehicle Industries
overview: The Vocational Pathways course develops students with the skills, qualities and qualifications needed to progress onto Level 1 vocational programmes or into employment. On this course you will develop transferable skills and qualities such as leadership, effective communication, working with others and supporting own health and wellbeing. You will participate in a variety of challenging in college and community enterprise projects in order to hone your skills. This is a flexible course providing a personalised learning experience and opportunity to work on individual targets and focus on areas of development to support next steps the following year. Alongside your main programme you will also study English and maths, participate in work related activities, follow an enrichment programme and also spend one day a week following your chosen vocational pathway. By selecting the Motor Vehicle pathway, you will spend your time learning all the Motor Vehicle basics in our state-of-the-art Motor Vehicle workshop. You will have an introduction to brakes, suspension, engines and engine principles, learning the basics of the motor vehicle industry. You will explore engine components, stripping down and refitting an engine and measuring and fitting brake pads. You will also participate in activities to understand how a real-life garage works and engage in work-based activity around this field.

title: Motor Vehicle Level 1
overview: The Motor Vehicle Maintenance course develops students with the skills, qualities and qualifications needed to progress onto a Level 2 full time Motor Vehicle course or an apprenticeship. You will spend your time learning in-depth Motor Vehicle principles in our state-of-the-art workshop with an industry expert. You will learn maintenance theories and demonstrate this learning through challenging practical activities in the workshop. Understanding and following health and safety will be a key feature of your learning and you will be expected to work at industry standard throughout your time, developing skills around professionalism and customer care. Working with real cars in a fully functional workshop you will learn how to strip and complete a full engine refit and understand the various components of an engine and their function. You will also learn the principles of brakes, suspension and tyres and put theory into practice in these areas. Alongside your main programme you will also study English and maths (where needed) and participate in work experience and work-based activity to underpin your learning in the workshop. This will include visits to local businesses and opportunity to meet industry experts to support your progression and transition the following year.

title: Multi-Channel Marketer Apprenticeship Level 3 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.Marketing is essential to small, medium and large businesses across a range of sectors and industries. Therefore, this apprenticeship will open doors to you in a range of businesses and organisations to suit your interests. In the role of Multi-Channel Marketer, you will work with either a team who deliver marketing desirables and advice to external clients, or within an internal marketing team, delivering marketing activities to drive business for that company or organisation. You will also be required to conduct market research to help shape marketing strategies and activities and contribute your ideas to a variety of marketing campaigns. You will be involved in content creation, data analytics, monitoring expenditure, distributing marketing content and literature, and you will be expected to take on responsibility as a first point of contact for the wider marketing team. On this apprenticeship, you will contribute to the implementation of the Marketing strategy or plan for the company you work for. You will be responsible for delivering marketing activities across a range of platforms, channels and systems used by the company to promote their business and services to clients. As a multi-channel marketer, your employer will define your responsibilities but these will branch out across a range of marketing roles. This could include digital marketing (email, website, social media, PPC); copywriting, PR and advertising; data analysis and performance monitoring and much more.

assessment: During the course of the apprenticeship, you will be assessed using witness testimonies, project work, written reports and assessment, reviews and online portfolio. The final end point assessment completes the apprenticeship with professional discussion and a project.

title: Multi-Channel Marketer Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: Marketing is essential to small, medium and large businesses across a range of sectors and industries. Therefore, this apprenticeship will open doors to you in a range of businesses and organisations to suit your interests. In the role of Multi-Channel Marketer, you will work with either a team who deliver marketing desirables and advice to external clients, or within an internal marketing team, delivering marketing activities to drive business for that company or organisation. You will also be required to conduct market research to help shape marketing strategies and activities and contribute your ideas to a variety of marketing campaigns. You will be involved in content creation, data analytics, monitoring expenditure, distributing marketing content and literature, and you will be expected to take on responsibility as a first point of contact for the wider marketing team. On this apprenticeship, you will contribute to the implementation of the Marketing strategy or plan for the company you work for. You will be responsible for delivering marketing activities across a range of platforms, channels and systems used by the company to promote their business and services to clients. As a multi-channel marketer, your employer will define your responsibilities but these will branch out across a range of marketing roles. This could include digital marketing (email, website, social media, PPC); copywriting, PR and advertising; data analysis and performance monitoring and much more.

title: Music Level 3
qualification: L3 BTEC NATIONAL FOUNDATION DIPLOMA IN MUSIC (19+ LOAN)
level_badge: Level 3 National Foundation and Extended Diploma in Music
overview: On this course, you will develop your knowledge and understanding of the music sector and gain valuable experience of music production and performance. The course has four mandatory modules across two years of study which are: Music Skills Development or Production Skills Development, The Music Industry, Personal Music Profile and Collaborative Music Project. Throughout the course, you will deepen your knowledge of music history and theory, using your knowledge to enhance your music performance and composition. You will also develop your music production skills and use our specialist equipment at college to create your own DAW project. Professional and employability skills are also a key part of this qualification and across the two years you will undertake a range of collaborative and independent projects that will help you develop your organisational and technical skills and enhance your professional practice and conduct.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 5 GCSEs with the following grades: English at 4 or above and maths at 3 or above and 3 other GCSEs at 4 or above.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.

career_opportunities: Students can seek employment in roles such as: music producer, freelance musician, writer, manager, events coordinator and technical support.
assessment: This course is assessed internally through a series of units that are graded on elements of both performance and written elements.

title: Painting and Decorating Level 1
overview: Learning takes place through a blend of classroom teaching and workshop-based activities, giving you real experience of working within the trade environment. A strong emphasis is placed on understanding and applying health and safety regulations. You will learn how to work safely and responsibly—protecting yourself, your colleagues, and future customers—while gaining an awareness of the legislation that governs professional practice in the industry. As part of your practical development, you will learn to apply paint systems to non complex areas, hang foundation and plain papers, and produce specialist decorative finishes. These activities are designed to help you build accuracy, confidence, and attention to detail in your work. Within our workshops, you will develop the core skills needed for employment, including how to prepare work areas, use tools correctly, and follow industry-standard processes. You will also gain experience in setting up and taking down access equipment and working platforms, preparing surfaces, and applying different finishes using materials and techniques relevant to the trade. Throughout the programme, you will deepen your knowledge of materials, tools, and methods used across the sector and develop the professional skills essential for progressing into further training or entering the workforce.
