title: Personal Trainer Apprenticeship Level 3 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.You will need to secure employment before starting this apprenticeship. When you are not at work, you will study at college to learn the theoretical side of personal training. This includes expanding your knowledge of physical training, nutrition and anatomy, physiology and kinesiology. Another key aspect of personal training is being aware of current government guidance on health and wellbeing, as well as a good base knowledge of human biology. This will enable you to give your clients researched and safe advice on diet and exercise. You will also learn how to use a range of technologies used in personal training, such as pedometers, apps and heart rate monitors to support your clients and tailor their diet and exercise routines. Throughout the apprenticeship, through both you’re on job learning and college lessons, you will gain the knowledge, skills and behaviours needed to become a successful personal trainer. This includes learning how to build a client base through good marketing practices and reputation of developing personalised exercise programmes. You will have lots of support from your employer and your college tutors throughout the apprenticeship programme, and you will have regular one-to-ones to support you through the programme and end-point assessment (EPA).
entry_requirements: A Level 2 Fitness Instructing or other related qualification and maths and English GCSE at grade 4/C. To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: You will be able to enter employment as a Personal Trainer or apply for degree-level courses related to sport, fitness and nutrition.
assessment: Your End-Point Assessment will include practical observation with questions and answers, presentation with questions and answers, and an interview.

title: Personal Trainer Diploma Level 3
overview: If you are passionate about health, fitness, and exercise, then this industry ready course is a perfect fit. By completing this qualification, you will meet industry requirements to become a REPS recognised personal trainer. On conclusion of this course, you will have acquired the competencies to work unsupervised as a personal trainer. This includes being able to offer one-to-one training, baseline assessment, nutritional advice and progressive programming which is specific to individual needs of a client. On the course, you will examine key principles of nutrition to support a client’s health, promote and encourage healthy lifestyle behaviours and long-term change, design and implement effective training sessions for a range of clients meeting client expectations. You will also develop an understanding for building a business acumen for working in both an employed role and for self-employment. Alongside your studies, you will gain real life experience of gym operations by supporting the staff team running the Kirklees College Waterfront Gym.

title: Physical Preparation for Entry to the Uniformed Protective Services Level 2
overview: Being part of the protective services can be both exciting and challenging at times. This course has been designed to challenge you to become our next police officers, fire fighters and ambulance crew members. This is a hands-on course. You will participate in a number of command and control practices, crime scene investigations, outdoor activities, navigation and expeditions to acquire and nurture core skills required in the uniform service. You will evolve your team work, communication, professional, technical and physical skills that will be transferable to your role within the public and uniformed services and allow you to excel within the industry. You will be presented with the opportunity to visit both military and emergency services to get a first-hand experience of what it is like to work within this fast paced industry.
entry_requirements: A minimum of four GCSEs with the following grades: English at 3 or above and maths at 2 or above and two other GCSEs at 3 or above.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Uniformed Protective Services Level 3
career_opportunities: This course is a great choice and an excellent stepping stone if you are enthusiastic about a career in uniform protective services. It is also ideal for preparing learners for progression to Level 3 course of study. The course is dedicated to skill development and preparation for next steps, supporting career journeys in the police, health service, fire service and armed forces.
assessment: There are no formal exams throughout this course. You will be heavily assessed on your ability to demonstrate skills and behaviours through observations and completion of logbooks and diaries.
additional_information: Students will be required to have a Kirklees College UPS uniform to participate on this course. This can be purchased from gbkits.co.ukIf you are worried about the cost of this kit then students can apply for support via the student bursary scheme.

title: Plastering Apprenticeship Level 2 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: To be able to start a plastering apprenticeship, you need to be employed for a minimum of 30 hours a week and attend college one day a week. Plasterers apply internal and external finishes to walls and ceilings to create a variety of required textures, as well as producing casting moulds for decorative features. In theory lessons, you will develop your understanding of health and safety in the workplace as you look at the various industry related regulations. You will be taught about construction technology and how to interpret drawing and work out quantities for pricing work.While in the workshops you will learn to apply different external finishes including pebble dashing and plastering curved surfaces. In our plastering bays, you will develop your skills using a range of materials. You will continue to develop the skills throughout the duration of the course. You will look at more complex practices, such as running in-situ moulds, applying plastering materials to detailed interiors, applying plastering materials to detailed exteriors, producing reverse moulds for detailed fibrous plaster and cement casting, producing and fixing detailed fibrous plaster and cement casts. Our Plastering Team maintain strong links with the various employers and work closely with British Gypsum and Weber who supply materials and site visits. You will have the opportunity to compete in regional Skill Build competition with previous students going on to compete at national level against other college students.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths at grade 2 or above. To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.

career_opportunities: Plasterer
assessment: The achievement of knowledge, skills and behaviours within the training plan whilst carrying out off-the-job training (OFTJ). The completion of a portfolio of work-based evidence.
additional_information: You will be required to wear safety footwear in the workshops at all times.

overview: If you wish to start a new career in construction and have little or no experience, the Entry Level 3 Award in Plastering qualification has been developed for delivery in a training environment; this means that you do not need to be employed to study this qualification. You will also learn to prepare to carry out Plastering, how to clear and store Plastering tools and use specialist equipment in a safe and efficient manner. title: Plastering Level 1
overview: The plastering industry continues to grow, and so does interest in our plastering course. This course has practical lessons based in our workshops and plastering bays and theory lessons to provide you with the skills required to learn your chosen trade. Theory lessons will cover the health and safety regulations in the workplace and how you need to apply them to your workstations and the wider building environment. To be successful in the building trade, you need to understand the importance of planning and communicating across teams, which you will be taught in theory lessons. Working in our plastering bays, you will learn how to prepare a background, apply plaster to various backgrounds and produce components from moulds. Getting your plaster mix right is key and you will be taught the calculations required to get your mix just right for different materials. This course is supported by strong employer links, with British Gypsum and Webber providing supplies for the course and talks to our students. You will have the opportunity to apply to compete in the regional Skill Build competition with previous students competing at national level.

title: Plastering Level 2
overview: This course combines practical sessions working in our plastering bays with theory lessons to help develop your underpinning knowledge of the skills required to be a successful plasterer and to develop your understanding of the construction industry. In theory lessons, your understanding of health and safety in the workplace will be developed. You will be taught about construction technology and the importance of communication on a site. While in the workshops, you will learn to form different types of screed and plaster materials. Your theory classes will help to understand how to calculate mixtures for different materials. In our plastering bays, you will develop your skills using a range of materials, as well as continuing to develop basic skills such as plastering on to stud partitions and applying render materials to external surfaces. You will also start to work on more complex forms of plastering skills including patching, cornice work and mouldings and fibrous plaster components. Our Plastering Team maintain strong links with the various employers and work closely with British Gypsum and Weber who supply materials and site visits. You will have the opportunity to compete in regional Skill Build competition with previous students going on to compete at national level.

additional_information: You need to have safety boots for the start of this course. You are not allowed in the workshops without them.

title: Plumbing and Heating Apprenticeship Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: To start an apprenticeship you need to be employed for at least 30 hours a week and able to come to college one day a week. Plumbing and Domestic Heating Technicians plan, select, install, service, commission and maintain all aspects of plumbing and heating systems. Customer service skills and being tidy and respectful are key as you could be working in customers’ homes or on building sites. As part of your apprenticeship, you will also learn about electrical components and control systems on plumbing and domestic heating systems. Our tutors will also develop understanding your of environmental technologies like heat pumps, solar thermal systems and bio mass and teach you about working in an efficient and economical manner to minimise waste. As an apprentice, you will learn about accurate measuring, marking, cutting, bending and jointing metallic and non-metallic pipework. You will be taught about core plumbing services which includes planning, installation, testing, commissioning and de-commissioning. As well as service, maintenance, fault diagnosis and repair techniques on cold water, hot water, central heating, above ground drainage and rainwater systems. As with all construction trades it is important that you understand health and safety legislation, codes of practice and safe working practices. You must be able to demonstrate that you can understand scientific plumbing, domestic heating and mechanical principles by the end of your apprenticeship. As your work will often involve working in a domestic setting, you will be taught customer service skills and the importance of good communication skills.
entry_requirements: A minimum of grade 4 in GCSE English and maths.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: A Level 4 qualification, professional qualification or full time employment.
assessment: To achieve your apprenticeship you must complete a portfolio of work, but written and practical examples. As part of end point assessment you will be required to complete a skills task, on-line assessment and participate in a professional discussion that includes a workplace observation.

overview: This qualification focuses on practical skills and knowledge and combines classroom and workshop sessions. It provides all the basic domestic heating and plumbing training you need to start work in the industry. On this course, you will learn how to understand and carry out safe working practices and gain an understanding into electrical systems used within the plumbing and heating industry. You will learn how to install and maintain domestic heating systems. On this course, you will learn how to work on domestic hot and cold-water systems. You will also look at different pipework fabrication techniques and learn how to use the appropriate tools and equipment. You will also get the opportunity to learn how to install and maintain above ground drainage systems and rainwater systems. Plumbers often work in people’s homes, so you will also be taught the importance of good communication and customer service skills.

title: Postgraduate Diploma in Education (Lifelong Learning) Level 7
qualification: This qualification is awarded by the University of Huddersfield.
overview: The Postgraduate Certificate in Education (PGCE) – Lifelong Learning validated through the University of Huddersfield and is designed for graduates who aspire to teach in the post-compulsory education and training sector, such as Further Education (FE) colleges, adult and community education, work-based learning, and more. This course is aimed at those who already hold a degree and want to pursue a career in teaching or training learners aged 14 and above. It combines academic study with practical teaching experience, focusing on evidence-based practice, professional reflection, and inclusive education in diverse lifelong learning contexts. The PGCE Lifelong Learning consists of three compulsory 30-credit modules studied at Level 7 (Masters level): Research Informed Teaching, Learning and Assessment; Becoming a Subject Specialist Teacher, and Being a Subject Specialist Teacher. You will then complete a fourth 30 credit module from the following: Curriculum and Context; EDI and Social Justice: Curriculum and Context; English as an Additional Language: Curriculum and Context, or SEND: Curriculum and Context. The Teaching Practice Requirement for this course is: a minimum of 250 hours of practice, of which at least 150 hours must be direct teaching; at least 20 hours must be completed in a different teaching context (second placement); teaching diverse learners across a range of subjects, levels, and contexts, and online teaching (up to 20 hours) may be included.Formal written assignments will be uploaded to Brightspace to be checked against Turnitin software. In completing the module assignments and observations you will be able to show that you are addressing the Learning and Skills Teacher Occupational Standard along with a number of themes which are seen as essential elements within the professional repertoire and activity of a teacher or trainer:Behaviour Management; Equality and Diversity and Social Justice; Subject specialist pedagogy; English and Maths; Digital Literacies and Professional Development.
entry_requirements: A UK first degree at 2:2 or above in a relevant subject area, and Level 2 qualifications in English and Maths (e.g. GCSE Grade C/4 or equivalent. You must have a placement or employment that supports the course’s teaching requirements (for part-time and in-service), and show commitment to working in the lifelong learning sector. A satisfactory Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) check must also be obtained.
fees_funding: £4,250 per year. You could pay for this course using a learning loan. Further information is available at Student Finance England.
career_opportunities: This course will open further opportunities in FE colleges, Sixth Form Colleges, adult education, work-based training and more.
assessment: You will be assessed through observations, summative and formative assessment methods.
qualification_awarding_body: This qualification is awarded by the University of Huddersfield.

title: Practical Animal Care Skills Level 3
overview: Taught at our specialist Taylor Hill Animal Centre, the Practical Animal Care Skills Level 3 course is designed to equip you with the hands-on skills necessary to care for a range of animals. You will complete a range of modules that cover caring for, handling and treating animals to meet their complex needs. Our Taylor Hill Animal Centre has a brand-new Reptile and Aquatics Centre, aviary, small mammal house, and mini farm filled with species from across the world! All your learning will be hands-on and you will get to know all the animals as you grow and develop your practical animal care skills. You will learn how to plan appropriate animal handling and ensure the safety of animals during movement control. You will also learn about animal diets and how to monitor and evaluate the feeding of animals. This course is ideal for anyone looking to start a career in animal care and wants to gain a thorough understanding of caring for a range of species.
entry_requirements: A minimum of four GCSEs at grade 4 and above, including maths and English.
progression: You can progress to HNC Animal Management, university, employment or further study.
career_opportunities: You can pursue a range of careers, such as Animal Technician or Zookeeper.
assessment: You will be assessed through practical and written assignments.

title: Preparation for an Apprenticeship
overview: The Level 2 Preparing for an Apprenticeship course is a brand-new programme designed to support learners with SEND and/or an Education, Health and Care Plan (EHCP) to successfully progress from education into an apprenticeship or employment pathway. This course provides the skills, confidence and real-world experience needed to prepare for an apprenticeship, with specialist support throughout your journey. This programme is designed to help you build confidence, develop workplace skills and gain valuable experience with real employers. With personalised support and hands-on opportunities, you’ll be well prepared to apply for an apprenticeship in your chosen career area. During the programme, you will achieve a Level 2 qualification in Employability Skills, attend bespoke GCSE classes in small, supportive groups, receive one-to-one support from a specialist Job Coach, complete a two-week placement with an apprenticeship provider linked to your career interests, and access expert careers advice and guidance to suport your apprenticeship applications. By the end of the course, you will have the skills, experience and support needed to progress into an apprenticeship programme in the following academic year.
entry_requirements: You will need a minimum of grade 2 in English and maths.
progression: You will progress to an apprenticeship, or other college course.
career_opportunities: This course will enable you to secure an apprenticeship in a field of your choice, which will be the first steps in building a career.
assessment: You will be assessed through coursework. You will also resit your English and maths GCSE if required.

title: Preparation For Supported Internship Level 1
overview: The course provides you with opportunities to develop your work experience and employment skills in a few different settings. Placements involve being mentored externally in the work place while benefiting from specialist job coaches, pastoral support and tutor-led sessions in college. You will spend a day a week in college or on industry visits and participate in two days of placements throughout the week. These roles have been selected to ensure tasks can be easily broken down and mastered with support. We have carefully built up our relationships with external providers to maximise student success and progression. Students need to be prepared to undertake the placements offered and have a positive attitude and a willingness to work. Job roles are selected to facilitate realistic progression to employment after the course by ensuring students can master a few key aspects of a role. Some students will be encouraged to rotate to a different role later in the year if they need an additional challenge. The course focus is on achievable placements which include grounds maintenance, cleaning, waitressing, stock control, post deliveries and waste management. Skills developed in both the classroom and on placement include time management, basic budgeting skills, following instructions, CV creation and updating, job search, dealing with conflict, asking for support, staying safe and developing greater independence. To help you manage the move into employment, you will benefit from access to experienced pastoral support and a tutorial programme.
entry_requirements: There are no formal entry requirements. You will take part in an interview to see if you have a demonstrable desire to work and to see if you have any additional support needs.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Progression onto a supported internship, part-time or full-time paid employment or voluntary work.This course has been designed as a middle entry point or progression route on the Pathway to Employment.
career_opportunities: Employment of 16 hours or more.
assessment: You will be assessed via feedback from your employers, reviews in the workplace, performance in team building activities, mock and real interviews and exams in English and maths where applicable.

title: Prepare for English GCSE
overview: Unable to start your English GCSE before the end of September? Don’t worry because with our intensive seven-week course, we will teach you the foundations of the GCSE, in preparation for the Fast Track GCSE. This course is both a perfect refresher course for anyone having recently studied GCSE English Language, or a great introduction to the basics of the English Language GCSE for those who are less confident.Class times: Fridays 09:30-12:45To join this course, please either e-mail info@kirkleescollege.ac.uk or ring 01484 437070.
fees_funding: Free if you don’t already have a Level 2 Functional Skills qualification or a grade 4 or C or above at GCSE, or if you have been assessed below a Level 2 or grade 4 GCSE.

title: Preparing for Self-Employment
level_badge: Level 1 Award in Preparing for Self-Employment
level: Level 1 Award in Preparing for Self-Employment
overview: Are you thinking about becoming your own boss? Have a business idea but not sure where to start? Join our Level 1 Award in Preparing for Self-Employment and take your first steps with confidence. You will also build confidence and develop essential enterprise skills, and access support, guidance and local business networks. Whether you’re planning to start a small business, become a sole trader, or turn a hobby into income, this course will give you the knowledge and tools you need. This practical course will help you understand what’s involved in starting and running a small business, explore business ideas and identify your strengths, and learn about budgeting, planning and basic marketing. Classes start Monday 26 January, and run until Monday 15 June. Sessions run 9.30am – 12.30pm. To join this course, please email ace@kirkleescollege.ac.uk, or call 07356148327.

title: Principles of Business Administration
qualification: L2 CERTIFICATE IN PRINCIPLES OF BUSINESS ADMINISTRATION (LEARNING CURVE)
overview: This course is designed to provide learners with the underpinning knowledge required by employees who are working or wish to work in a business administration role. This course can be completed at your own pace using your own online learning space, with the added benefit of tutor feedback and support as you complete the course tasks. This qualification on your CV will enhance your opportunities, and prepare you for further study in this field. The course content includes understanding communication in a business environment, employer organisations and understanding how to carry out business administration tasks. During your studies you will gain understanding in the principles of document production and information management. The course takes an average of 10 weeks to complete but if you are working at your own pace you have the flexibility to complete the course sooner. Upon completion of this course you may wish to progress onto our Team Leading or Customer Service courses.

title: Production Chef Apprenticeship Level 2 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: Production chefs often work in kitchens where large quantities of food are required, and on a tight schedule, such as in hospitals, care homes, schools, the Armed Forces or high street casual dining kitchens. If you are highly methodical, organised and have the energy and cooking skills required for this kind of environment, this apprenticeship is the perfect way to start your catering career. Within your job, you will be working as part of a wider team of chefs and get a feel for working in this kind of environment. You will also gain vital professional skills such as working with a range of clients, effective communication and how to be compliant with current food hygiene and health and safety legislation. During your apprenticeship, you will learn how to maintain excellent standards of personal, food and kitchen hygiene; ensure compliance to procedures and recipes; produce food meeting portion controls and budget restraints, and use a range of specialist kitchen equipment. When you have your college lessons, you will attend lessons in our state-of-the-art kitchens at our Huddersfield Centre.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths at grade 3 or above.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Employment
career_opportunities: This apprenticeship will qualify you to work as a Production Chef in a range of environments.
assessment: You will undergo End Point Assessment at the end of your apprenticeship.

It is for people already employed in accountancy who will have responsibility for creating and/or verifying and reviewing accurate and timely financial information within the organisation in which they are employed or on behalf of another organisation. On this course you will look at management accounting, financial statements of limited companies and accounting systems and controls. You will also develop your understanding of business and personal taxation, ethical standards alongside regulation and compliance. While on this apprenticeship you will look at management accounting, financial statements of llimited companies and accounting systems and controls. You will also develop your understanding of business and personal taxation, ethical standards alongside regulation and compliance.

title: Professional Cookery Level 2 (Adult Route)
overview: Our chef lecturers will develop your practical skills in food preparation and cooking. Lessons take place in our industry standard kitchens, and in Landings 72 our restaurant that is open to the public. Our level 2 students run the afternoon service of the restaurant, working in the production kitchen and front of house. As well as developing your practical skills, it is important that you learn about food safety and hygiene alongside health and safety in the industry. The course also helps you develop your understanding of catering operations alongside learning about costings and menu planning. All students will look at healthier food and developing both menus and dishes for special diets. In the kitchens our Chef lecturers will develop your skills, including the preparation and cooking of meat, fish, poultry, vegetables, soups, stocks and sauces. Once you have mastered the basics, we expand your skills to look at more complex dishes for example cooking shell fish and offal. In our bakery you will develop your baking skills looking at hot and cold desserts as well as producing biscuits, cakes and sponge products. Utilising industry links, students will get to experience visiting guest Chefs taking their lessons. All students are encouraged to enter externally organised competitions as well as taking part in our in-house Hell’s Kitchen competition. Students also work on live briefs designing specific products or menus.
entry_requirements: Completion of a written piece or work indicating you are working at an appropriate level to successfully complete the course. An interest, or previous experience in catering. Age 19+If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: L3 Advanced Diploma in Professional Cookery Kitchen and Larder or Pastry and Confectionary
career_opportunities: Employment within the catering industry in a variety of settings.
assessment: You will be assessed with written work, presentations, practical assessment of your skills and assignments demonstrating your subject knowledge and skills.

title: Professional Cookery Level 3
overview: On this course, you choose one of the specialist routes: kitchen and larder or pastry and confectionary. Chef lectures take place in our industry standard kitchens. On this course, you will also be responsible for running our restaurant, Landings 72, which is open to the public during the day and one evening a week. This will include menu planning and running the production kitchen and front of house. For both pathways, you will study three core units: the principles of food safety supervision for catering, supervisory skills in the hospitality industry and practical gastronomy. You will develop the necessary knowledge and understanding of the principles of the dining experience. If you choose to follow the kitchen and larder pathway, then you will learn advanced skills and techniques in producing vegetable and vegetarian, meat dishes, poultry and game, fish and shellfish dishes. On the pastry and confectionary route, you will produce dough and batter products, petit fours, pastry products, hot, cold and frozen desserts, biscuits, cake and sponges, display pieces and decorative items. You will have the opportunity to enter a range of external competitions, including the prestigious Toque d’Or. You will benefit from visiting guest chefs and participating in live briefs. For the last two years students on this course have provided the food for the VIP guests at the Tour De Yorkshire.
entry_requirements: A minimum of GCSE English and maths grade 4 or above and a level 2 relevant vocational qualification.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Level 4 Diploma in Hospitality ManagementEmployment
career_opportunities: You could progress on to higher education or seek further employment.
assessment: You will be assessed by a mixture of presentations, practical skills assessments and assignments. You may also have to take part in professional discussions.
additional_information: You will be required to wear chef whites and have your own knives. These can be purchased from college.

overview: On this course, you will gain practical skills in food preparation, cooking and food and beverage service through training in the college’s purpose-built kitchens and restaurant, giving you greater confidence for working in the industry. title: Professional Graduate Certificate in Education (Lifelong Learning) Level 6
qualification: This is for you if you are:
overview: The Professional Graduate Certificate in Education (PGCE) Lifelong Learning validated through the University of Huddersfield and is designed for those wishing to teach in the post-16 education and training sector.This includes Further Education (FE) colleges, sixth-form colleges, adult and community education, work-based learning, and more. The course is underpinned by the Learning and Skills Teacher Occupational Standard and emphasizes reflective practice, inclusive pedagogy, and evidence-informed teaching strategies. You will develop both generic and subject specialist teaching skills. You will study four 30-credit modules, typically at Level 6 (Graduate level), including: Research Informed Teaching, Learning and Assessment; Becoming a Subject Specialist Teacher; Being a Subject Specialist Teacher. You will also complete one of the following, depending on availability and focus area: Curriculum and Context; EDI and Social Justice: Curriculum and Context; English as an Additional Language: Curriculum and Context, or SEND: Curriculum and Context. The Teaching Practice Requirement for this course is a miinimum 250 hours of practice (150 teaching hours). Your formal written assignments will be uploaded to Brightspace to be checked against Turnitin software. In completing the module assignments and observations you will be able to show that you are addressing the Learning and Skills Teacher Occupational Standard along with a number of themes which are seen as essential elements within the professional repertoire and activity of a teacher or trainer: Behaviour Management; Equality and Diversity and Social Justice; Subject specialist pedagogy; English and Maths; Digital Literacies; Professional Development.Classes will on on Tuesdays 17:00-20:30.
entry_requirements: You must have a UK Honours Degree (Level 6 or equivalent) in a subject that aligns with your intended teaching specialism, and Level 2 qualifications in English and Mathematics (e.g., GCSE Grade C/4 or equivalent). You must also currently be in a teaching placement or employment in a setting that allows you to meet the course teaching requirements (if in-service or part-time pre-service). You will also need a satisfactory DBS (Disclosure and Barring Service) check and health clearance and evidence of commitment to working in the post-compulsory education sector.
fees_funding: £4,250 per year. You could pay for this course using a learning loan. Further information is available at Student Finance England.
progression: You can progress to QTLS through the Society for Education and Training (SET), or Master’s-level study (e.g., Postgraduate Diploma in Education, MA Education).
career_opportunities: Careers in teaching are varied and rewarding. Once fully qualified you can teach students aged 14 upwards in secondary schools, the Further Education sector or Higher Education.
assessment: Assignments, observations, presentations

title: Professional Patisserie and Confectionary Level 3
overview: You will be working in our industry standard kitchens at our Huddersfield Centre to develop your craft over two years. On the course you will complete mandatory modules that will teach you how to produce dough and batter, petits fours, paste products, hot, cold and frozen desserts, biscuits, cakes and sponges, as well as display pieces and decorative items. Professional standards are expected at all times in our kitchens and as part of this we ask all students to purchase uniform and kitchen equipment before they start their course. You will be sent more information about how to order your kit before you start your course and financial support is available to help you with the costs if you qualify. Throughout your modules you will also cover the principles of food safety and hygiene, and learn how to work effectively in a professional kitchen. At Kirklees College we operate a student-led restaurant, Landings 72, which is open to the public. You will help prepare desserts, pastries and cakes for the restaurant, café and deli which will equip you with work skills to prepare you to work in a real kitchen environment. At the end of the course you will have extensively practiced a range of recipes which will prepare you to take the next steps in your career as a pattisiere.

career_opportunities: This course will qualify you to work as a pattisiere but possible other career paths include baker, chef, restaurant/café manager and other Catering roles.
assessment: You will be assessed through written assessment, practical tasks and synoptic assessment.

title: Professional Patisserie, Confectionary and Cafe Culture Level 2
overview: This course offers you the opportunity to develop your kitchen skills in the specific areas of patisserie and confectionary. You will also learn about front of house and beverage services. Your lessons will take place in the bakery, production kitchen and in Landings 72, our restaurant that is open to the public. Outside of the bakery, you will spend time working in Landings 72 learning about the customer side of running a café/restaurant. You will develop your front of house skills through studying customer service and barista training. The course also teaches you the importance of dish planning, costings and general operations. Our chef lecturers will teach you basic bakery skills, such as baking biscuits, cakes and sponges. You will also learn more complex skills like making petit fours, paste products and display pieces and decorative courses. As part of the course, you will also develop your skills to produce hot, cold and frozen desserts, alongside dough and batter products. You will get access to talks from visiting chefs and others who work in the catering industry to share their expertise and knowledge. Previous visiting chefs have included a master chocolatier and the owner of a successful outside catering company.

title: Progression Entry 3
overview: This course offers a flexible learning programme designed to help you improve your skills, confidence and independence and achieve a qualification in English and maths. The course will cover academic skills including English, maths and ICT which will be developed through practical tasks. You will undertake work-based learning and team work through team activities, community projects and food preparation sessions. Individual tutorials will allow staff to track and monitor your progress and you will also have access to social, emotional and pastoral wellbeing support. This programme has been designed to bridge the gap between secondary school and college courses at Level 1 and above. You may have a specific learning difficulty or disability, behavioural or social need, been a school refuser or are an anxious learner. An initial assessment will help to build up your individual profile and set realistic targets for you to work towards. The course will also focus on developing your skills outside the classroom, including self-assessment and career progression skills to allow you to make action plans. You will develop your employability skills, including job search and interview skills, as well as making CVs and completing application forms. There is also a programme of enrichment activities including art, yoga, reading and walking groups.

course_code: STEP INTO FE (ENTRY 3 LEVEL)
meta_description: This course is an ideal starting point for students who have achieved low GCSE grades, but who want to be able to progress into a Level 1 course.

title: Progression to Access To HE Level 2
overview: This course is ideal if you want to change career or need a path to university after time away from education and prepares you for a level 3 access course. You will study relevant units across humanities, health sciences and social sciences at level 2 to prepare you for your chosen level 3 access course. We will also support you to complete GCSE English or maths if required. The course also covers vocational subjects to improve your overall understanding in a variety of areas, including nutrition, performance and healthy eating, physiology and exercise, sociology of health and psychology, as well as mental health and ethical issues in social policy. The course comprises personal and academic units of study to develop your professional skills, including practical presentation, academic writing, IT and research skills, alongside developing a personal portfolio and understanding prejudice, discrimination and diversity within society.
entry_requirements: Either English or maths at grade 4 along with three other grade 4s or a Level 1 qualification with English and maths.Applicants must be 19+.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.

title: Project SEARCH Supported Internship
overview: Project SEARCH is a full-time one-year supported internship programme. It is a highly successful transition to work initiative for students aged 18-24 with a learning disablity and/or autism. It is targeted at students whose goal is competitive employment within the local community. Kirklees College has partnered with Kirklees Council, Dewsbury District Hospital, REAL Employment and C&K Careers to facilitate the licensed DFN Project SEARCH course. Throughout the year, you will have three work site rotations to help you build the skills required gain and maintain employment in your chosen sector. Rotations are matched to the your career aspirations, existing skills and focus on developing and personal development goals. Your tutor and Job Coach will work alongside you every day to support you in your development towards meaningful employment. The aim of Project SEARCH is to support you in your first steps towards gaining meaningful employment of 16 hours or more. Project SEARCH is five days a week, Monday – Friday. Each day you will spend time learning an employability curriculum, developing vital employability skills, such as communication, team work, self-advocacy and technology skills, as well as CV writing and interview preparation. Upon successful completion of Project SEARCH, you will then continue to receive support through REAL Employment. Over 60% of students graduate with employment of 16 hours or more. Past students have gone on to work in Business Support roles within Kirklees College and Kirklees Council. As well as other students gaining employment as Care Assistants, Activities Co-ordinator, Bus Driver and much more!
entry_requirements: There are no formal entry requirements. You will take part in an interview to see if the course is right for you and to see if you have any additional support needs. Students will need to have an EHCP, please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: This is the final stage of the employment pathway and students will be supported to find employment of 16 hours or more.
career_opportunities: This course is designed for students on an employability pathway. Students will be supported to apply for employment during the course of their studies and receive ongoing support from REAL Employment.
assessment: You will attend regular progress and development reviews with your tutor, family, job coach, rotation manager and mentor.

title: Salon Management
qualification: VTCT Level 4 Diploma in Salon Management
overview: Kirklees College is excited to offer this must-have qualification for established industry professionals. The course emphasises practical management skills, including financial planning, marketing, and staff development that will prepare you to become a salon manager. You will also cover Sales Management: strategies for increasing salon revenue and performance, and Public Relations (PR) in Hair and Beauty: managing marketing, branding, and PR campaigns.This course will run 1 day per week. Your key modules include: Salon Management: developing leadership skills, managing team dynamics, and delegation. Quality Management of Client Care: ensuring high standards in the hair and beauty sector. Management of Health, Safety and Security: maintaining a secure and compliant working environment. Assessment on this course will involve building a portfolio of work, practical observations, and theoretical assignments. You will be taught by industry professionals in our state-of-the-art salon based at Huddersfield Centre, where you will get a sense of what it is like to own and manage a salon, and apply your learning in a practical environment.

title: Site Carpentry Apprenticeship Level 2 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: To be an apprentice you need to be employed for a minimum of 30 hours a week and attend college one day a week. The apprenticeship involves carrying out skilled work, primarily using timber products, either on a construction site, or in a workshop, creating and installing building components. On your apprenticeship, you will learn how to install door and window frames, door and hatch linings and straight staircases. You will also learn how to repair or replace frames, mouldings, doors, windows, and door and window ironmongery. You will also be taught about inclined roofs with gables, joists, roof coverings and roof components. You will be taught how to use, maintain and store hand tools, power tools and associated equipment. A Site Carpenter will normally work on a building site, or in domestic and commercial premises, preparing and fixing building components, from the initial erection of a new building, through to the installation of all necessary fixtures and fittings, as well as a range of repair and maintenance activities. You will be taught about the importance of working safely and to be aware of key health, safety and welfare issues. By the end of your apprenticeship, you will be expected to be able to plan and carry out work to a commercial standard including using drawings and specifications to complete your work and understand how to select the required materials to carry out the work including timber, tools and fixings.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths at grade 2 or above. To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Level 3 Craft Carpentry ApprenticeshipEmployment
career_opportunities: As a qualified site joiner with specialist skills you could access a wide range of careers working on building sites for both domestic and commercial properties, as part of a companies estates team or for a specialist bespoke company. Many site joiners set up as self employed.
assessment: You will be required to achieve the knowledge, skills and behaviours within the training plan whilst carrying out off-the-job training (OFTJ) and the successful completion of a portfolio of work-based evidence.End Point AssessmentOnce all assessments are completed the apprentice will go through the final stage of the apprenticeship which will involve a multiple-choice exam and one day practical assessment.
additional_information: As a Site Carpenter, you need to be prepared to work in all weather conditions and be physically fit. You will need to provide your own safety boots.

title: Skills for Creative Industries Level 1
qualification: Gateway Qualifications Level 1 Diploma in Skills for Creative Industries
overview: This is a great entry level qualification if you know you want to progress to a higher, more specific Creative Industries qualification at college, but aren’t sure which area you want to study. By allowing you to explore a range of creative skills in Art and Design, Media and Music, you can decide on your specialism whilst gaining a valuable qualification. Employability skills are also built into the qualification and you can study a range of units that will help you prepare for the world of work. These include: applying for a job, career planning and interview skills. Students on this course must complete 42 credits during this one-year course. Modules include a range of creative skills, including acting, artwork, photography and more. Depending on which modules you study, you will be learning at either our Huddersfield Centre or Springfield Sixth Form Centre, where you will be taught by industry experienced tutors. English and maths skills are also built into the curriculum and will help you boost your knowledge of these key areas, alongside studying for your GCSEs, if you have not already achieved your grade 4.
entry_requirements: Four GCSEs at grade 2 or above in English and/or maths and an art-based subject. If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.

career_opportunities: This qualification prepares you for further study or work within the creative sector. Some careers in the sector include: photographer, graphic designer, freelance musician, actor, production assistant, broadcasting and marketing.
assessment: Throughout the qualification, you will be assessed via an ongoing portfolio of evidence.

title: Sport and Physical Activity - Sports Performance Extended Diploma Level 3
overview: Are you driven by performance, fascinated by the science behind elite athleticism, and ready to take your skills to the next level? This advanced two-year course is designed for students who want to pursue a career in elite sport performance, therapy or sport science. In your second year you will undertake a research project, which will require you to investigate key topics in sport and physical activity to deepen your understanding and critical thinking. You will also cover: Biomechanics, Applied Exercise Physiology, Sports Nutrition, Athletes’ Lifestyle, Sport Injuries, Analysis of Sports Performance, Work Experience in Sport, and Applied Strength and Conditioning. In your first year you will cover Anatomy & Physiology, Physiology of Fitness Exercise, Laboratory Experiment Methods, Exercise, Health and Lifestyle, Preparing for a Career in Sport & Physical Activity, Instructing Physical Activity and Exercis, Organising Sports Events, Fitness Testing for Sport & Exercise and Practical Team Sports. You will benefit from the specialist guidance of our industry-trained tutors and world-class facilities at our Huddersfield Centre.
entry_requirements: A minimum of five GCSEs including English and maths at grade 4, and three other GCSEs including Science and PE. If maths or English are not attained at a grade 4, you will be placed on the Level 3 Introductory Certificate, rather than a Level 2 qualification.
progression: You can progress to higher education or employment in the industry.
career_opportunities: You can progress into roles in Sports Performance management, training and physical therapy.
assessment: You will be assessed through written and practical assignments.

title: Sport Coaching and Physical Activity Introductory Certificate
overview: Are you ready to lead, inspire, and make a difference through sport? Our NCFE Introductory Level 3 Sport – Sport Coaching course is your gateway to a rewarding career in sport, physical activity, and leadership. On the course, you will cover Sports Coaching, learning how to plan, deliver, and review coaching sessions that motivate and develop others. You will also delve into the principals of Sport Leadership, developing confidence and leadership skills to guide teams, run events and inspire participation. This course is ideal for you if you are an aspiring coach, or fitness professionals. We are looking for students who are passionate about sport and helping others, and want to become future leaders in health, wellbeing and physical activity. Another key element of the course will be preparing for a career in sport and physical activity, and your tutors will help you explore career pathways, build your CV, and gain the skills employers are looking for in the sport and fitness industry.

title: Sport Leadership and Coaching Level 2
overview: This course is a great choice and an excellent stepping stone if you are enthusiastic about a career in sports coaching and leadership. It is also ideal for preparing learners for progression to Level 3 course of study. This course is dedicated to skill development and preparation for next steps. On this course, you will be encouraged to participate in a wide variety of sport, sports coaching and leadership scenarios. We have excellent links with local sports facilities, schools and sports teams to maximise your practical application and experience. You will get to enjoy learning and playing sport in a fun and friendly environment learning from experienced highly qualified coaches and tutors. During the course you will develop technical ability of coaching sports, advance your tactical awareness of different sports, whilst acquiring core skills, behaviours and attributes to be a successful leader. Additionally, you will learn the principles of planning and exploit your organisation competences by delivering successful activities and sporting events. We encourage our students to further their own development by accessing and undertaking extra qualifications in coaching, leadership and first aid. Alongside your course, you can combine learning and playing by joining one of our sports academies for football and rugby league in partnership with Huddersfield Giants.

title: Sport, Health and Physical Activity – Football Pathway Level 3
overview: The Football pathway of the Level 3 Sport, Health and Physical Activity course at Kirklees College will equip you with all the relevant knowledge and skills to pursue a career in the international football industry. You will be taught by tutors who have experienced in the football industry, and access to amazing opportunities such as oversees training trips, a day in the life experience at Manchester City, and the chance to become an Association of Colleges (AOC) Football Ambassador. In your second year, you will cover athlete’s lifestyle; technical and tactical skills in sport; sports nutrition; analysis of sports performance; current issues in football; work experience in football; sports injuries; fitness training, and strength and conditioning. In your first year, you will cover exercise, health and lifestyle; preparing for a career in football; football coaching; leadership in football; coaching specialist populations; organising sports events; instructing physical activity and exercise, practical team sports and fitness testing for sport and exercise. The fitness testing module is delivered in partnership with the University of Huddersfield, and you will have access to their world-class facilities. As part of this course, you will benefit from our industry partnerships and opportunities to enrich your experience. These include our partnership with West Riding, who you will work on a Your Game event with, Futsal officiating, using Catapult PlayerTek analysis and much more.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 5 GCSEs with the following grades: English at 4 or above and maths at 3 or above and 3 other GCSEs at 4 or above including PE or a sports background. If maths or English are not attained at a grade 4, you will be placed on the Level 3 Introductory Certificate, rather than a Level 2 qualification.
progression: You can progress to higher education or employment in the sector.
career_opportunities: This qualification will unlock routes to multiple careers in the football industry, including player, coach, physio and business and management roles.
assessment: You will be assessed through discussions, group presentations and activities, written assessments, practical demonstrations, fitness tests, logbooks and diaries and observations.

You will explore sporting tactics, rules and regulations understand the importance of a healthy lifestyle, promote improvement strategies and investigate current issues and barriers for physical activity. title: Street Food
overview: Experience the best of global cuisines as we take you on a culinary adventure through different flavours each week. On this course, we provide all the necessary ingredients for each session, eliminating the hassle of sourcing and shopping. Join us for a thrilling journey into the world of street food, where you’ll gain invaluable culinary skills and expand your knowledge of global cuisine. Each session promises to be an unforgettable experience as you delve into the vibrant tapestry of street food culture. Enrol in our 6-week Street Food Course today and let your taste buds travel the world! Engage in lively discussions with your fellow food enthusiasts, as together, you decide what exciting dishes to cook each week. We believe in giving you the freedom to explore and experiment with flavours that excite your taste buds.Sessions will run on Thursdays, 6pm – 8.30pm.
fees_funding: This course costs £75 to cover ingredients, apron included.

title: Supply Chain Practitioner Apprenticeship Level 3 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.Supply chain practitioners will work in one or more supply chain functions. They will forecast and plan customer demand, liaise with suppliers around production schedules, process orders and work with haulers and distributors in areas of transport planning. On this apprenticeship, you will also develop in-depth knowledge on the laws, policies and procedures that influence the supply chain. You will also learn about the IT systems used for supply chains, including business planning software. You will also learn continuous improvement management, problem solving and principles of buying such as risk, cost, laws, ethics and customer needs. Areas of study on this apprenticeship will include how to measure performance in a supply chain such as stock levels, accuracy of forecasts, customer service, on shelf availability of products and levels of waste. You will also learn how to forecast customer demand, considering factors such as weather, market trends, seasonality and competitor activity. Supply chain practitioners also must develop behaviours including pride in work, ensure safe working, be responsive to change and be a highly effective communicator.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths grade 4 or above and a suitable employer.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Management or specialist roles
career_opportunities: Purchasing Assistant, Logistics Controller, Team Leader, Supply Chain Operator/Manager, Shipping Department, Dispatch, Production Manager, Operations Management
assessment: You will be assessed by your assessor using a portfolio of evidence, practical demonstrations and assignments. You will be assessed in your place of work.
additional_information: To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable for a minimum of 30 hours a week.

title: Supply Chain Warehouse Operator Apprenticeship Level 2 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.As a Warehouse Operative, your duties will include taking deliveries, checking for damaged/missing items, storing goods, moving stock by various methods, picking/packing orders, loading goods for dispatch, maintaining stock records, documentation and cleaning. This apprenticeship also involves understanding how to use basic IT, including barcoding, data capture, radio and warehousing management software and systems. You will gain an understanding of how to communicate effectively with colleagues, customers, third party carriers and other organisations. You will learn how to use warehouse equipment, machinery and vehicles, including mechanical racking systems and materials handling equipment, such as forklifts and pallet trucks. The apprenticeship will take a minimum of 12 months to complete prior to taking the end-point assessment.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths grade 3 or above and a suitable employer.
progression: Higher level apprenticeshipEmployment
career_opportunities: After this apprenticeship you can progress into a range of role including; Purchasing Assistant, Logistics Controller, Team Leader, Supply Chain Operator/Manager and operations management.
assessment: An assessor will visit you in the workplace and will assess your through observation, discussion, product evidence, assignments and witness statements. Assessors will require access to observe your skills, attributes and competencies in the workplace. This will be necessary and used to assess your competence in your job role.
additional_information: To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable for a minimum of 30 hours a week.

title: Supporting Teaching and Learning Diploma Level 3 (September 2026 Entry)
overview: This course has now started for academic year 2025/26.The course will provide you with an in-depth understanding of the knowledge and skills needed when working directly with children and young people in school and college environments, covering all aspects of specialist support. You will support assessment for learning and engage in professional development. You will learn more about supporting speech, language and communication, as well as how to support bilingual learners. You will understand how to support those with special educational needs and young people’s transitions through each stage of their education. You will also support the role of play, leisure and extra-curricular activities. You will understand schools and colleges as organisations, as well as children and young people’s development. You will learn how to safeguard children and young people and maintain relationships with them. You will support the health and safety of children and positive behaviour in a learning environment. You will learn how to support learning activities and English, maths and ICT in a learning environment. All units will be internally assessed using a range of methods which could include: written assignments or tasks such as designing a leaflet or booklet, charts or diagrams or setting up an information board. You will also be required to undertake 6-10 hours of independently identified placement on a weekly basis.
entry_requirements: The level 2 Supporting Teaching and Learning in Schools qualification and a minimum of level 2 English and level 1 maths. A DBS check and 10 hrs per week placement/employment in a relevant setting is also required.
fees_funding: The cost of this course is £2,225 To find out if you are eligible for funding, please email customerservices@kirkleescollege.ac.uk

title: T Level Animal Care and Management Level 3
overview: During this two-year course, you will mix classroom and practical learning with nine weeks of compulsory work placement in the animal care industry. Our work experience team at college will help you find a suitable placement which will give you vital sector experience alongside your lessons in theory. You will cover a broad range of topics related to the animal care sector, including sustainability and climate change, the principles of biosecurity, the supply chain and principles of stock management and business organisations, enterprise skills and project management. After you complete this T Level, you will be able to apply for jobs in the sector directly or progress onto an apprenticeship or university to enhance your qualifications.
entry_requirements: A minimum of five GCSEs at grade 4 or above including English, maths and preferably science.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Animal Management HNC/HNCUniversityEmploymentFurther Study
career_opportunities: After this course you can pursue many careers in animal care, including zoo keeper, animal care worker, assistance dog trainer, dog handler, horse riding instructor and more.
assessment: Your core knowledge and skills will be assessed through exams and an employer-set project. Assessment of occupational specialist knowledge and skills will vary depending on the occupation. It may involve tests, practical assignments and projects. Your occupational specialist knowledge and skills will be assessed in the second year.

title: T Level Building Services Engineering for Construction: Electrotechnical Engineering Level 3
overview: On this course, you will cover topics that are relevant to the electrical industry to prepare you for a future career in electrical installation. Whilst on your two-year course, you will learn about the engineering processes and procedures for electrotechnical systems. The course will also provide you with real-life practices to use in future roles, such as building design, surveying and planning, and the building technology principles which are standard practice in the construction industry. The units on this course will also cover the design and planning of electrical systems and their components. You will gain an understanding on construction in general by learning about a number of issues that are relevant to the sector, such as health and safety when working with electrotechnical systems, building regulations and standards and construction methods. The course will also provide you with real-life practices to use in future roles, such as building design, surveying and planning, and the building technology principles which are standard practice in the construction industry. The units on this course will also cover the design and planning of electrical systems and their components. This qualification focuses on a wide range of green technologies that are emerging in the electrical industry. You will look at sustainability in the construction industry and how it will apply when working on electrotechnical systems. You will also undertake a minimum of 315 hours work in an industry placement in the second year of your studies. We will support you in finding a placement.
entry_requirements: GCSE maths or Science at grade 5 and four other GCSEs at grade 4 or above to include English, maths and science. If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: HNC Electrical/Electronic EngineeringApprenticeshipEmployment
career_opportunities: Electrician, Electrical Engineer, Site Supervisor
assessment: Synoptic practical assessment, Theory-based assignments, Employer-set project
additional_information: At the start of your course, you will need a pair of steel toe cap boots that must be worn at all times.

title: T Level Digital Software Development Level 3
overview: T Levels are unique as they integrate mandatory work placement with a full Level 3 qualification, which can get you into university, a degree apprenticeship, or help you go straight into the world of work. This Pearson Level 3 Digital Software T Level is no exception, and is perfect for anyone looking to start a career in the digital sector. At Kirklees College, you will benefit from having help to secure an industry placement with one of our employer partners, under the guidance of our Work Placement team. Alongside them, and your tutors, you will be supporting through every step to find a great work placement to compliment your classroom learning. On this course, you will study a range of modules related to digital software development, including: problem solving, programming, emerging issues and the impact of digital, legislation and regulatory requirements, business context, data, digital environments and security. This course will equip you with a foundation of digital software development skills to help you decide what specialisms you would like to pursue in this field.
entry_requirements: A minimum of GCSE English at grade 5 or above, and four other GCSEs at grade 4 or above to include maths.
progression: You can progress to university level study, a higher apprenticeship or employment in the digital sector.
career_opportunities: Job roles you can progress to include web developer, web designer, software developer, computer games tester, computer games developer, e-learning developer, and user experience (UX) designer.
assessment: You will be assessed through a mix of written work, an employer set project and exams.

title: T Level Education and Childcare: Early Years Educator Level 3
overview: On this course, you will cover themes that are relevant to the education and childcare industry to prepare you for a future career in early years education or childcare. Whilst on your two-year course, you will develop an understanding of the education and childcare sector from ages 0 to 19. Your industry placement will consist of a minimum of 750 hours of work during your two years of studies and you will be supported in finding your placement. You may either be working on your own or supervising others to deliver the Early Years Foundation Stage (EYFS) requirements set by the government for the learning, development and care of children from birth to 5 years old. You will learn how to develop relationships with children to facilitate their development, the importance of safeguarding, health and safety and wellbeing for children. You will also learn how to plan, provide and review care, play and educational opportunities to enable children to progress and develop as they grow. You will study the importance of working in partnership with colleagues, parents, carers and other professionals to support children’s development. The course will provide you with real-life practices to use in future roles, such as supporting and promoting children’s play, development and early education. You will be able to practice your activity plans and demonstrate your skills and knowledge in preparation for observation in setting in our new nursery room areas within college.
entry_requirements: A minimum of five GCSEs with the following grades: five GCSEs at grade 4 or above, including English Language and maths. You will also need to have a fully enhanced DBS check.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Foundation DegreeHigher Education
career_opportunities: You can progress to employment as an Early Years Educator or full-time employment in the early years and education sector.
assessment: You will be continually assessed through written assignments, presentations and a range of other formative and summative assessment methods. You will undertake two external examinations and an employer set project. You will also be assessed throughout your placement by a workplace assessor.

title: T Level Education and Early Years: Assisting Teaching Level 3
overview: In the first year of the T Level, you will learn about a range of core knowledge and skills related to the Early Years sector, ages 0 to 19. You will have access to our mock nursery and be taught by industry experts to develop your skills in a dynamic, practical environment. You will need to secure a work placement and over the two-year qualification will complete 750 hours of mandatory placement. During this time, you will be supported to develop plans and opportunities to respond to children’s needs, including those with SEND. In your second year, you will complete the assisting teaching occupational specialism pathway. This pathway will enable you to become proficient in supporting the class teacher to enhance children’s education, individually or in groups whilst planning in collaboration with teachers and other adults.

meta_description: Our exciting new T Level Education & Early Years: Assisting Teaching is suitable for anyone wanting a rewarding career as a teaching assistant. It is equivalent to three A-Levels and is delivered through a combination of classroom-based study and work placement within an industry setting.

title: T Level Engineering and Manufacturing: Manufacturing, Processing and Control Level 3
overview: On this course, you will study a specialist machining route alongside the core units to prepare you for a future career in engineering, manufacturing, processing and control. The qualification will give you a detailed insight into the engineering industry. This technical qualification focuses on the development of knowledge and skills needed for working in the engineering industry, which will prepare you to enter the industry through employment or as an apprentice. You will also have access to cutting edge manual and CNC machinery and CAD facilities, as well as tuition from industry experienced lecturers. You will cover topics such as: processes of production and manufacturing, materials used in production, manufacturing and fabrication environments, specialist machinery utilised in the production and manufacturing environments and product and project management. In the second year, you will be supported in finding an industry work placement (minimum of 315 hours), to develop your knowledge and understanding of working as an engineer further and strengthen your future prospects of employment. You will be able to apply the core learning from your course to workplace scenarios during your placement.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 5 GCSEs with the following grades: English at 4 or above and maths at 5 or above and 3 other GCSEs at 4 or above including a science or technology course.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: HNC/HND, Apprenticeship, Degree
career_opportunities: This qualification allows you to progress into roles such as a mechanical engineer, electrical and electronic engineer or civil engineer.
assessment: You will be assessed through synoptic practical assessment, theory-based exams and an employer-set project.
additional_information: You will be required to wear all necessary personal protective equipment provided by the college when working in our engineering workshops.

meta_description: This exciting new qualification has been designed to give you the dynamic skills needed for a career in a range of engineering, manufacturing, processing and control occupations.

title: T Level Engineering: Maintenance, Installation and Repair for Engineering and Manufacturing Level 3
qualification: T Level Technical Qualification in Maintenance, Installation and Repair for Engineering and Manufacturing
overview: This qualification is designed for 16–19 year olds who want a practical route into engineering. It is ideal for students interested in how mechanical, electrical and control systems work together, and supports progression into skilled employment, a higher or degree apprenticeship, or further technical study in engineering and mechatronics. You will also complete a minimum of 315 hours work placement with an employer as part of this T Level programme. This will allow you to further your practical skills learnt at college in a real-world environment, and develop your professional behaviour. You will study core engineering knowledge alongside specialist mechatronic content, focusing on integrated systems used in industry. You will develop skills in maintenance planning, fault diagnosis, installation and repair, safe working practices and professional behaviours, combined with a substantial industry placement to build real world experience using industry standard equipment. You will be taught by industry experts at our state-of-the-art Engineering Centre. The multi-million centre has the very best and most up-to-date equipment and tools required to prep you for a career in mechatronics engineering.
entry_requirements: Five GCSEs at grade 4 or above, including maths and English at grade 5. A science, engineering, or technology subject at Grade 4 or above is also strongly preferred. You must also have a clear interest in engineering and technical problem solving abilities.
progression: You can progress to a higher education course, an apprenticeship, or directly into employment.
career_opportunities: Job roles include Maintenance Engineering Technician, Mechatronics Technician, Electromechanical Engineer, Automation or Controls Technician, Manufacturing Engineering Technician, and Field Service Engineer. These roles are typically based in manufacturing, processing, utilities, transport, or advanced engineering environments.
assessment: The course is assessed through two written exams, a real world employer set project, and a practical occupational specialism assignment. A minimum 315 hour industry placement is also required to build workplace skills, though it is not graded.
