title: T Level Foundation Year - Early Years Practitioner Level 2
overview: On this course, you will cover 14 mandatory units that are relevant to the education and childcare industry to prepare you for a future career in early years education or childcare. Whilst on your course, you will develop an understanding of the education and childcare sector from ages 0 to 19. During your time on the course you will complete two placements in two different settings, which could include full day care, children’s centres, pre-schools and reception classes. You may either be working on your own or supervising others to deliver the Early Years Foundation Stage (EYFS) requirements set by the government for the learning, development and care of children from birth to 5 years old. The T Level Foundation course will cover various subjects related to child development, safeguarding, play, learning and assessment. You will be expected to demonstrate and apply your knowledge and skills in placement and also within your own individual portfolio. You will be expected to communicate and interact effectively within a team using a full range of skills. You will learn the importance of working independently and taking responsibility for initiating and completing tasks and understanding the impact of your work on others within an early education setting.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 4 GCSEs with the following: English at 3 or above and maths at 3 or above and another 2 GCSEs at 3 or above. You will also need a DBS check.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: Early Years Educator T Level Level 3Early Years Educator Apprenticeship
career_opportunities: You can progress to a career as an early years practitioner, nursery nurse, teaching assistant or employment within the early years and education sector.
assessment: You will be continually assessed through written assignments, presentations and a range of other formative and summative assessment methods. You will also be assessed in your placement by a workplace assessor.

title: T Level Foundation Year Animal Care Level 2
overview: This course will help to develop your knowledge and skills of general animal care, which is vital for anyone wishing to work within the animal care sector. You will also complete a module of learning that prepares you to progress to the T Level in Animal Management at Level 3. The course also involves a unit which allows you to develop your knowledge and skills in animal grooming. You will focus on grooming dogs and skills in bathing, health checks and grooming. The animal collection at Taylor Hill Animal Centre is varied and you will develop skills in handling, welfare and health of the different types of animals, for example, reptiles, birds and small mammals. You will learn about the design and requirements of animal accommodation and undertake practical sessions such as cleaning, health checks and feeding to ensure the animals are safe and healthy.

progression: Animal Care and Management T Level
career_opportunities: You could progress to employment within the animal care and management industry, including animal collections, kennels, catteries, pet shops, animal welfare establishments, animal grooming parlours and veterinary nursing.
assessment: You will be assessed through written assignments, presentations and practical work.

title: T Level Foundation Year Business Level 2
qualification: L2 FOUNDATION T LEVEL - DIPLOMA IN BUSINESS
overview: This one-year course has been designed to give you the best chance of progressing onto your chosen T Level. You will gain the technical knowledge and practical skills, as well as working towards GCSE English and maths and digital skills at a higher level. You will learn how trends and the current business environment may impact on a business and then you will plan an idea for a new business, which will help you to develop a better understanding of the costs involved in a business and how they can make a profit. You will also explore the role of marketing and consider how businesses use market research to make marketing decisions. This course will provide you with the necessary skills to develop a good understanding of the business environment and the different functions that operate within a business. This qualification has been developed with leading businesses, schools and colleges to help you gain the knowledge, skills, and work experience that you will need to be successful in the workplace. There will be opportunities to build your employability skills by participating in live briefs delivered by external companies, such as Lloyds Banking Group, National Market Trade Federation and Kirklees Council.

progression: You can progress to the Level 3 T Level in Management and Administration: Team Leadership and Management.
career_opportunities: Once you have completed the Level 3 T Level, you can progress to various roles in the business sector, including Business Development Manager, Order Management Coordinator, Business Administrator and Customer Services Manager.

title: T Level Foundation Year Engineering Level 2
overview: This course will provide you with the knowledge and skills for direct entry to the T Level in Engineering. As well as giving you time to develop your knowledge of key maths and science principles applied to engineering, you will learn and experience a wide range of engineering disciplines. You will study hand fitting and assembly techniques, manual turning, manual milling, computer aided design, electrical and electronic principles and engineering design techniques. You will be provided with all health and safety equipment and PPE, including workshop overalls and safety boots. You will study a mixture of practical and theoretical topics whilst developing key manufacturing skills and engineering knowledge which are all essential for successful study at T Level. The T Level in Engineering, Manufacturing and Processing and Control is a highly challenging and yet rewarding study programme that gives you a broad base in engineering theory with the application of knowledge through practical tasks and a substantial work placement. This transition pathway will prepare you for the demands of the T Level course whilst giving you a broad experience of a variety of engineering disciplines and careers.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 4 GCSEs with the following grades: English at 3 or above and maths at 3 or above and 2 other GCSEs at 3 or above including a science or technology course.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.

career_opportunities: You can to progress to a career as a Mechanical Engineer, Electrical Engineer, Electronics Engineer, Mechatronic Engineer, Machinist, Design and Development Engineer, Fitter, Engineering Technician or Civil Engineer.
assessment: You will be assessed through an online test, written exams, practical tests.

title: T Level Foundation Year Health Level 2
qualification: L2 FOUNDATION T LEVEL IN HEALTH AND SCIENCE - PROGRAMME AIM
level_badge: Level 2 T Level Health and Science
overview: This course is specifically designed to prepare you to join the T Level Health cohort after one year of study and will also provide you with a Level 2 qualification. You will cover core principles of health and social care practice and ethics, giving you a good foundation of knowledge to progress in the field. You will also work with your Personal Development Coach to find a suitable work placement during your course. This will help you to develop your practical skills which are essential in the Health and Social Care sector, and prepare you for the extensive work placement hours embedded in the T Level. The core content you will cover on this course encompasses equality, diversity and rights in health and social care; human growth and development through the life span, and the role and responsibilities of the health and social care practitioner in relation to safeguarding individuals. You will continue to work on your maths and English skills using Century, an online portfolio and this will be built into your study programme. If you do not already have a grade 4 in English and / or maths then you will also continue to study towards these vital qualifications. You will also study for a GCSE in Biology as part of this course, as this is a key requirement for the T Level programme.
entry_requirements: A minimum of four GCSEs with the following grades: English at 3 or above and maths at 2 or above and two other GCSEs at 3 or above. A DBS check is also required.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: This qualification will allow you to progress to either the T Level Health: Supporting the Adult Nursing Team or T Level Health: Supporting the Mental Health Nursing Team.
career_opportunities: Care Support Worker, Healthcare Assistants, Community-Based Support Worker, Nursing.
assessment: You will be assessed through a portfolio of evidence.

title: T Level Health - Supporting the Adult Nursing Team Level 3
overview: On this course, you will cover topics that are relevant to the health industry to prepare you for a future career in adult nursing. Whilst on your two-year course, you will study the general regulation and processes in the health sector, such as managing information and data and working in the sector. For some of these modules, you will work alongside our T Level Science students. This course will also cover how you care for adults, aged 18 to elderly people, in a variety of settings. This includes for patients all with different levels of dependency. You will also learn how to undertake a range of physiological measurements on patients to ensure you are providing a high level of care, as well at look at an individual’s overall care and wellbeing. The course will also provide you with real-life practices to use in future roles by covering topics that are specific to health to support your future career. This includes focusing on understanding the healthcare sector, how to provide person-centred care so it can be used in everyday practice and infection prevention and control. On the course, you will also undertake a minimum of 315 hours work in an industry placement. 100 hours will be undertaken in your first year of study, with the remaining 215+ hours completed in your second year of study. Your placement will be split to give you real experience in the health industry. You will be supported in finding your placement.
entry_requirements: A grade 5 in English Maths or Science in addition to 4 Grade 4s. You must have English, maths and science as part of this combination, and one of these subjects must be at Grade 5. You will also need to have a DBS check.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: On completion of this course, you can progress to higher education in the healthcare sector, an apprenticeship or employment.
career_opportunities: You can progress to employment or an apprenticeship.
assessment: Paper A written examinationPaper B written examinationEmployer set projectSynoptic assignments

title: T Level Management and Administration: Team Leadership and Management Level 3
overview: On this course, you will cover topics that are relevant to the business industry to prepare you for a future career in management and administration. Whilst on your two-year course, you will study business and commercial awareness. You will develop an understanding of project and change management, including common change management theories and models, which will help you support and improve business projects. This T Level is exam-based in your first year, so you will be preparing to sit two core knowledge exam papers, which will be sat in June. You will have an employer-set project in your first year, which contains controlled assessments. You will also undertake an industry placement, which will start towards the end of your first year and continue into the second year of your studies. The course will also provide you with real-life practices to use in future roles, such as working collaboratively with others. You will also understand the importance of good communication and how to adapt your communication in a business environment. You will learn how to change your behaviour when speaking to different audiences depending on the context. The UCAS points you gain on this course will be attached with a value equivalent to three A-levels, meaning the completion of this qualification will allow you to progress to higher education courses and training.

title: T Level Science - Laboratory Sciences Level 3
overview: On this course, you will cover topics that are relevant to the science industry to prepare you for a future career in laboratory science, including cell cycle and cellular respiration, scientific methodology, experimental equipment and techniques and ethics of science. The level of science undertaken in this T Level is A-level standard and will cover all three sciences, biology, chemistry and physics. The course will also provide you with real-life practices which you will be able to apply to future job roles. This includes focusing on learning the science sector, further scientific knowledge used in everyday practice and developing relevant analytical techniques. You will study general regulations and processes, managing information and data and working in the sector. You will study this core content with students on our Health T Level course. You will learn the principals of good scientific and clinical practice to prepare you for your industry placement which can be undertaken in various settings, including the NHS, schools, textile labs, quality control and chemical processing plant labs. The units on this course will also cover the ethics of science and how it can influence your work and the work undertaken by organisations. You will also look at the experimental equipment and techniques and how they can affect processes within laboratory sciences. This will help you identify and resolve issues with scientific equipment or data errors.
entry_requirements: 1 GCSE Grade 5 and 4 GCSEs grade 4 or above. You must have English, maths and science as part of this combination, and one of these subjects must be at Grade 5.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: On completion of this course, you can progress to higher education, employment or an apprenticeship.
career_opportunities: You can progress into roles such as a laboratory technician, technical support scientist and laboratory assistant.
assessment: Synoptic Practical AssessmentTheory ExamsEmployer-set project

title: Team Leader / Supervisor Apprenticeship Level 3 (In-Year Start)
overview: *Please note: We can only accept applications from individuals who have already completed Year 11 (or equivalent), as this course begins before September 2026. If you have not yet finished Year 11, please apply for September 2026 entry instead.This apprenticeship is suitable for team leaders in a first line management role with responsibilities for operational matters, a given project, or managing a team to deliver a clearly defined outcome. You will provide direction, instructions and guidance to ensure the set goals are achieved. It will typically take up to 15 months to complete the apprenticeship with a minimum of 20% off-the-job training. Exact duration will depend on your previous experience. During this apprenticeship programme, you will be introduced to a variety of management styles. This will assist you to effectively build relationships and motivate staff and colleagues. You will understand the tools to develop and mentor teams, allowing them to reach their full potential. You will be equipped with the ability to solve problems, manage resources, achieve goals and build stakeholder relationships. You will already be in employment and in a position to support, manage and develop team members, manage projects, plan and monitor workloads and resources.
entry_requirements: GCSE English and maths at grade 4.To start your apprenticeship you must have secured employment that the college has agreed is suitable.
progression: Register as a Associate member with the Chartered Management Institute and/or the Institute of Leadership & Management to support your professional career development and progression.
career_opportunities: This apprenticeship will support you progressing into management roles in a range of different business environments.
assessment: You will be assessed on your knowledge, skills and behaviour outcomes throughout the duration of the course with the final synoptic end point assessment.

title: Theatrical, Special Effects and Hair and Media Make-Up Level 3
overview: This technical based course will prepare you with all the required skills to work in the media, fashion and photographic industry. The course is designed to allow you to express your own creative flair for applying make-up and creating your own image. You will also have the opportunity during your studies to advance your skills in styling and fitting a postiche and camouflage make-up. You will also focus on client care so you are able to adopt a client-centred approach to all designs and treatments. You will gain the practical skills to airbrush make-up to your client’s face, develop images and designs to fit in the fashion and photographic industry and develop effective communication in the workplace, as well as the application of prosthetic pieces and bald caps. You will be able to learn within our purpose-built hairdressing and beauty salons stocked with all the essentials to create your desired looks. Our salons are open to the public, so you will be working within a professional environment from the start of your course. Students will practice on real clients and may also practice treatments on other students on the same course at Kirklees College.

additional_information: You will need to purchase both kit and uniform to be able to do this course. These are available from the college.

title: Travel and Tourism - Aviation Level 3
qualification: NCFE Introductory Diploma in Travel and Tourism: Aviation
overview: This course is an in-depth qualification that will equip you with all the knowledge, skills and behaviours you need to work in the Travel and Tourism Aviation industry. Over the two years, you will complete a range of modules covering the UK and International Travel and Tourism industry, as well specialist modules covering aviation. Each module on the course will introduce you to a different role within this industry, allowing you to understand the breadth of roles available and plan your career path once you leave college. Work Experience is built into your timetable at college and you will have lots of exciting opportunities to engage with major employers in the industry through talks, trips and work placement. The Travel and Tourism modules you will complete are: the UK Travel and Tourism Industry; Customer Service in Travel and Tourism; Preparing for a Career in Travel and Tourism and UK Visitor Attractions. You will develop your knowledge of the aviation sector and its job roles by completing these modules: Investigating Airline Cabin Crew; UK Airports; Worldwide Passenger Airlines; Airport Terminal Operations and Airport Ground Operations. As this course is preparing you for a professional role, you will be required to purchase a uniform for your studies. More information about costs, how to buy and support available will be communicated to you before you start the course.

progression: Training scheme within the aviation industry, apprenticeship or employment.
career_opportunities: People who complete this course go on to a number of roles, including cabin crew, ground control, airport terminal operator and travel agent.
assessment: You will be continually assessed throughout this qualification through a portfolio of evidence.
additional_information: You will be required to purchase a uniform for this course. More information about where to buy this and financial support will be sent to you before you start.

title: Travel and Tourism Level 1
qualification: NCFE Level 1 Certificate in Travel and Tourism
overview: This Level 1 course is ideal if you are looking to work in the Travel and Tourism industry. Over the course of the year you will cover the UK Travel and Tourism industry, customer service, sales, communication and marketing in your learning. This will give you a good grounding in the basic principles of working in the industry, allowing you to experience what a career in this sector could look like. As part of your study programme you will continue to develop your maths and English skills using Century, an online learning portal. If you don’t already have a grade 4 in English and/or maths then you will also continue to study for these qualifications. Preparing you for the world of work is at the core of this qualification and applying the knowledge you learn to a working environment is a core part of the qualification. Throughout the course you will learn how to work in a team, use ICT systems and practice customer service and professional conduct. You will be taught by industry experienced tutors who will bring a wealth of knowledge to your lessons and be able to pass on their knowledge and expertise.
entry_requirements: A minimum of four GCSEs with the following grades: English at 2 or above and maths at 1 or above and two other GCSEs at 2 or above. If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.

title: Travel and Tourism Level 2
qualification: NCFE Level 2 Certificate in Travel and Tourism
overview: This Level 2 course will provide you with an understanding of the principles of the travel and tourism industry and equip you with transferable skills to work in a number of roles within the industry. You will learn about the UK and international industry. You will study full-time at our Huddersfield Centre, where you will be taught by industry professionals and engage in a range of extra-curricular activities to deepen your understanding of Travel and Tourism. Travel and Tourism students also regularly engage with employers in the industry, as work experience is a key part of your studies, preparing you to work in this exciting world. All students on the NCFE qualification will undertake two core modules: Customer Service in Travel and Tourism, and the UK Travel and Tourism Industry. You will also learn about Travel and Tourism destinations, promotion in Travel and Tourism, UK purpose-built visitor attractions, hospitality in Travel and Tourism, career planning in the industry, and UK coastal tourism. Whilst on your study programme, you will continue to work on your English, maths and digital skills. If you have not achieved a grade 4 in your English and/or maths GCSEs, you will continue to work towards these qualifications.

title: Understanding Nutrition and Health
qualification: NCFE Level 2 Certificate in Understanding Nutrition and Health
overview: This is an online, self-led course.Nutrition is a fundamental aspect of health and wellbeing and understanding its principles is essential for anyone interested in promoting healthy living. Our Level 2 Certificate in Understanding Nutrition and Health is designed to equip you with the knowledge and skills needed to make informed decisions about your diet and to support others in achieving optimal health. The six mandatory units of this certification cover a wide range of topics, from the principles of healthy eating to the principles of food safety for the home environment. Unit 1, Exploring Principles of Healthy Eating, is an essential foundation for the rest of the certification. Unit 2, Considering the Nutritional Needs of a Variety of Individuals, builds on this foundation by exploring the specific nutritional needs of different individuals. Unit 3, Using Food and Nutrition Information to Plan a Healthy Diet, takes a practical approach by teaching you how to use food and nutrition information to plan and prepare healthy meals. Unit 5, Understanding Eating Disorders, is an essential component of this certification, as it explores a serious and growing public health concern. Unit 6, Principles of Food Safety for the Home Environment, is an important aspect of promoting healthy living. This unit covers the principles of food safety in the home environment, including how to handle, store, and prepare food safely.Before starting the course, you must complete a pre-assessment. This needs to be submitted at least two weeks prior to the enrolment start date to confirm your place on the course.
entry_requirements: There are no formal entry requirements for this course. However, we recommend that learners have an intermediate level of reading and writing in English. As the course is delivered online, basic digital skills will also be needed. Before starting the course, you will be asked to complete a pre-assessment. This needs to be submitted at least two weeks prior to the enrolment start date to confirm your place on the course.
fees_funding: The cost of this course is £380. To find out if you are eligible for funding, please email customerservices@kirkleescollege.ac.uk
assessment: This course is delivered purely online with digital content. Upon enrolment learners are sent login details for our e-assessor platform where they will access all course materials. To pass this qualification, learners are required to complete two formal assessments (one after each module) which are submitted to us for marking. Each unit will have a set deadline so Learners can plan their study time to fit in with these dates.
qualification_awarding_body: NCFE Level 2 Certificate in Understanding Nutrition and Health

title: Uniformed Protective Services (Emergency / Military Strands) Level 3
overview: In the first year, you will study mandatory generic subjects to provide you with essential knowledge, technical and practical skills required for work in the public service sector. You will study up to five units covering a diverse range of material including: leadership and teamwork, outdoor expeditions, physical preparation and citizenship. The units have been developed to challenge and excel your personal development. During the second year of this course you will have the option of selecting between our military or emergency service strand . This will enable you to hone in on bespoke subject areas and experiences to fully equip you for your next steps. Our tutors are from ex-service personnel providing a wealth of significant experience to supplement your learning. You will be presented with the opportunity to engage with both military and emergency services through, talks, visits, residentials and trips to get a first-hand experience of responding to emergencies and serving your community.

progression: Direct entry into the public services industryDegree level courses
career_opportunities: This course has been designed to support transition and prepare you for a fulfilling rewarding career in the police force, fire service, military and armed or protective services. If university is your next step, this course also equates to tariff points to support higher education study. The intent of this course is to help you become occupationally ready to take up employment in the uniformed protective services sector.
assessment: There are two formal exams throughout this course one in the first year and one in the second. Other methods of assessment include discussions, presentations, practical participation, written assignments, reports and portfolios.

meta_description: This course is a perfect fit if you have a work hard mentality.  The course is centred around the core values and standards of the industry. Professional behaviours and attitudes will be promoted throughout.

title: Uniformed Protective Services Level 3
overview: This course will allow you to explore your resilience, leadership and physical fitness whilst developing your confidence, communication and organisational skills through a combination of practical and theory-based learning. On this course, you will undertake a number of expeditions, where you will be required to plan and navigate routes across open country, interpreting route cards and putting survival skills into practice. The course is designed to assist you in the next steps of your career by allowing you to explore different careers within the uniformed services. You will devise an action plan and start working towards meeting the intended entry requirements for your chosen career route. Your problem-solving skills will be tested in a number of command task whereby you will be challenged to apply effective solutions to complex technical problems.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 5 GCSEs with the following grades: English and maths, one of which must be at a grade 4 or above and one at grade 3 or above, and 3 other GCSEs at grade 3 or above OR a relevant level 2 qualification with a pass grade and GCSE English and maths, one of which must be at a grade 4 or above and one at grade 3 or above, or equivalent.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.

title: Vehicle Maintenance & Repair Principles Diploma Level 4
qualification: This is for you if you are:
overview: If you are a technician with a Level 3 qualification, you will develop your knowledge and understanding of diagnosing motor vehicle faults in situations where no prescribed process or format is available. You will also develop your ability to provide technical support and advice to colleagues working in motor vehicle environments. You will also be trained in how to support diagnostic equipment and information systems in motor vehicle settings, as well as how to conduct diagnostic consultations with customers in these environments. Additionally, throughout your training, you will enhance your ability to liaise with vehicle product manufacturers and suppliers on technical matters. You will train at our Engineering Centre in Huddersfield, where you will have access to a state-of-the-art, full-scale Motor Vehicle workshop. Guided by experts, your training will be up-to-date and relevant to industry standards.

title: Vocational Pathways - Pathway to Animal Care and Land Based Studies Entry 3
overview: The Vocational Pathways course develops students with the skills, qualities and qualifications needed to progress onto Level 1 or Level 2 vocational programmes or into employment.On this course you will develop transferable skills and qualities such as leadership, effective communication, working with others and supporting your own health and wellbeing. You will participate in a variety of challenging in college and community enterprise projects in order to hone your skills. By selecting the Animal and Land Based Studies pathway, you will learn the basics of handling and taking care of different kinds of animals including their diet, health and wellbeing. You will also develop skills and knowledge through a variety of practical sessions at our Taylor Hill Animal Centre with our specialists to support your learning and understanding in this field. You will also spend one day a week learning at our Huddersfield Centre. This is a flexible course providing a personalised learning experience and opportunity to work on individual targets and focus on areas of development to support next steps the following year. Alongside your main programme you will also study English and maths, participate in work related activities, follow an enrichment programme and also spend time each week following your chosen vocational pathway. After you finish the course, you could be offered the chance to transition to a Level 1 programme within the Animal and Land Based department, or another college course of your choosing. A relevant qualification in English and maths will support this transition.

career_opportunities: Level 1 accredited courses, apprenticeship or employment.

title: Vocational Studies Business Level 1
overview: This course will allow you to develop the fundamental skills required within the Business sector. You will learn about business finance, promotion and market research and carry out practical projects that will develop your business skills. The course is designed to be taken over one year, giving you the opportunity to develop a range of skills in the business sector and support your progression to further study. Developing techniques that help organise your work and manage your time is key in business and is one of the attributes you will learn about, alongside promotions, market research, communication, budgeting, underpinning business values and what makes a business. You will also have the opportunity to research a topic, link it to a group topic and then present your findings. You will study a variety of units that will develop your knowledge and practical skills. The skills you develop will be transferable and sector-specific to provide you with the understanding of how a business works and awareness of how to progress within a business. You will carry out numerous practical projects that will develop your business skills. To help build your business skills, students have the opportunity to be involved in enterprise activities and projects. To contribute to your learning, you will have the chance to participate in discussions with outside agencies as part of our developed guest speaker programme.
entry_requirements: A minimum of 4 GCSEs with the following grades: English at 2 or above and maths at 1 or above and 2 other GCSEs at 2 or above.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.

assessment: You will be assessed by discussions, observations, group presentations, written assessments and group activities. Coursework will be set to reflect the practical skills learnt within class.

title: Web and Software Development Level 3
qualification: UAL Diploma in Creative Media Production
overview: By combining UI/UX design with Computer Science programming, this project-based course gives you the versatility to build high-performance software and stunning web applications from the ground up. You won’t just learn theory; you will research, design, and develop real-world solutions for live clients using industry-standard project management workflows. You will gain proficiency in professional tools including Figma, Adobe Photoshop, and Illustrator for design, alongside Visual Studio and Android Studio for development. Throughout the course, you will build a tangible portfolio, by working on themed projects and live client briefs to create a professional portfolio that proves your skills to future employers or universities. The industry has shifted. Employers no longer want just a coder or just a designer—they want “T-shaped” professionals who understand the full lifecycle of a product. We’ve integrated these disciplines to give you a broader skill set, allowing you to explore both fields before specialising. Universities and employers now place a premium on developers who understand user experience and designers who understand the architecture of code. In year one, you will build the foundations of your skills, diving into the basics of Web and Software Development, UI/UX principles, and digital design. This year is about building your toolkit and understanding how design and code interact. In second year, you will tailor your learning to your career goals, and move into advances territory of back-end development, mobile app development and complete your final major project.
entry_requirements: Five GCSEs grade 4 or above to include English and maths.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: This course is a direct route to University, Higher Apprenticeships, or immediate employment in the tech sector.
career_opportunities: Job roles you can progress to include web developer, software developer, amongst other careers in the digital sector.
assessment: You will be assessed through a mix of written assessments and project work, culminating in your final major project.

title: Women's Hairdressing Level 3
overview: This course has been designed to teach you all the skills required to work in the hairdressing industry, from the promotion of products and services to your clients to monitoring and maintaining health and safety practice within the salon. During the practical sessions, you will further develop your skills in cutting, styling, colouring and finishing your client’s hair, as well as providing specialist consultation services for hair and scalp treatments based on questioning, observations and test results. You will also learn about client care and client focused practice, as well as effective communication. You will study in our purpose-built hairdressing and beauty salons stocked with all the essentials to create your desired looks. Our salons are open to the public so you will be working within a professional environment from the start of your course. This course is recognised by the UK’s leading professional body (The Hairdressing Council). Students will practice on real clients and may also practice treatments on other students on the same course at Kirklees College.

title: Work Skills Level 1
overview: Work Skills provides a learning environment for every student that is personalised, inclusive, safe, enjoyable and challenging with appropriate support and guidance to enable them to develop academic, personal and employability skills. The course content includes English and maths, preparation for work experience placement, participation in a work experience placement, PAD, weekly work-based activity at Bradley Woods Activity centre and enrichment activities. Students will learn to identify their own strengths and areas for improvement whilst learning to recognise their own behaviours in challenging situations. Students will develop an awareness of social and cultural issues as well as learning about sources of help and support. The course encourages self-advocacy, team work, responsibility, self-management, communication, problem-solving and building positive relationships. This course is designed to meet the needs of individual students and has a strong practical focus. The course provides a unique opportunity for students who require a longer transition into college life. The focus is primarily on helping students to recognise the importance of education. We support students to develop personal, social, emotional and employability skills, engage in college life, prepare for progression and the next stages at college.
entry_requirements: You will take part in an interview to see if the course is right for you and to see if you have any additional support needs. You will need to be working at Entry Level 3 or above in English.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: A Level 1 course, vocational course in a variety of areas at level 1 or 2, apprenticeship or employment.
career_opportunities: This course is designed for students on an employability pathway. Students may be able to progress onto the Work Focus programme, vocational courses or gain employment with support from the employment support specialists.
assessment: Students will be assessed through their portfolio work, weekly work-based activity, contribution to discussions and presentations, observations of individual and group work, completion of mandatory work and exams in English and maths if appropriate.

title: Xplorer Programme Level 2
overview: The programme delivers a personalised, inclusive and safe learning environment where you will find your learning challenging, enjoyable and thought-provoking. A strong focus on personal goals and how to overcome barriers to learning will be embedded in all aspects of the course. You will work independently and as part of a team on a variety of social enterprise and charity activities to develop yourself in readiness for life and work. This will include participating in college and community events, using a kitchen space to budget and prepare meals and running a small business. Students on Xplorer will have underpinning careers guidance to support future career progression decisions. You will also learn about where to access different sources of help and support, identify your own strengths, skills, qualities and interpersonal skills and how these can be transferred into jobs or further education. The Xplorer programme is designed to meet the individual needs of students with a strong emphasis on developing personal, social, emotional and employability skills. The team offer a unique opportunity for learners with social, emotional or mental health needs to engage in learning within a nurtured environment and provides support to students to re-engage in their learning and prepare for next steps.
entry_requirements: You will take part in an interview with the course tutor to see if the course is right for you. Students usually require GCSE English and/or maths grade 4 or above.If you have an EHCP then please be aware that the college needs to have agreed, via the consultation period with your local authority, that we can meet the needs outlined in your EHCP before you can start on a course with us.
progression: After this course, you can progress onto a Level 2 or 3 full-time course at college of your choosing, apply for an apprenticeship, or go straight into the world of work.
career_opportunities: Further education, apprenticeship or employment.
assessment: You will be assessed through your portfolio work, completion of compulsory work-related activity and placement and exams in English and maths where applicable.
